revisions.content:
The '''Tesla Motor Company''' is a [[Silicon Valley]] startup company focusing on the production of high performance, consumer-oriented [[electric vehicles]]. The firm was started in [[2003]] by engineers [[Martin Eberhard]] and Marc Tarpenning, and has since grown to include several team members with extensive computer, electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds. Tesla has also managed to secure initial funding from prominent investors, such as [[PayPal]] co-founder Elon Musk, and Google co-founders [[Sergey Brin]] and [[Larry Page]].

In May 2006, the company issued a press release stating that it would reveal product details the following July. 

==External Links==
[http://teslamotors.com/index.html] Official Tesla Motors website

The firm was started in [[2003]] by engineers [[Martin Eberhard]] and Marc Tarpenning, and has since grown to include several team members with extensive computer, electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds. Tesla has also managed to secure initial funding from prominent investors, such as [[PayPal]] co-founder Elon Musk, and Google co-founders [[Sergey Brin]] and [[Larry Page]]. In May 2006, the company issued a press release stating that it would reveal product details the following July. The date for unveiling the first car - the [[Tesla Roadster]], was later confirmed to be July 20, 2006. ==External links== * [http://teslamotors.com/index.html] Official Tesla Motors website

'''Tesla Motors, Inc.''' is a [[Silicon Valley]] automobile startup company focusing on the production of high performance, consumer-oriented [[electric vehicle]]s. The firm was started in [[2003]] by engineers [[Martin Eberhard]] and Marc Tarpenning in [[San Carlos, California|San Carlos]], and has since grown to include several team members with extensive computer, electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds from the region. Tesla has also managed to secure initial funding from prominent investors, such as [[PayPal]] co-founder [[Elon Musk]], and [[Google]] co-founders [[Sergey Brin]] and [[Larry Page]].

Tesla has a signifigant relationship with [[Lotus Cars|Lotus]]. A design contest was held for the final design of the [[Tesla Roadster]], then nicknamed "[[Dark Star (film)|Dark Star]]", of which Lotus won. Lotus will build the roadster, which is similar in size and appearance to their [[Lotus Elise|Elise]].

'''Tesla Motors, Inc.''' is a [[Silicon Valley]] automobile startup company focusing on the production of high performance, consumer-oriented [[electric vehicle]]s. The firm was started in 2003 by engineers [[Martin Eberhard]] and Marc Tarpenning in [[San Carlos, California|San Carlos]], and has since grown to include several team members with extensive computer, electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds from around the world. 

Initial investment came from [[PayPal]] co-founder [[Elon Musk]], who became Tesla's Chairman of the Board, leading Tesla's first two rounds and co-leading the third together with VantagePoint Venture Partners. Tesla's third round also included investment from prominent investors, such as [[Google]] co-founders [[Sergey Brin]] and [[Larry Page]], and former [[eBay]] president, [[Jeff Skoll]].

Tesla has a signifigant relationship with [[Lotus Cars|Lotus]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''', then codenamed ''"[[Dark Star (film)|Dark Star]]"'', which Lotus's design studio won. Lotus will assemble the roadster, which is similar in size to their [[Lotus Elise|Elise]]. Tesla is currently working on a sedan, codenamed ''"[[White Star (automobile)|White Star]]"'' which may be introduced as early as 2008.

'''Tesla Motors, Inc.''' is a [[Silicon Valley]] automobile startup company focusing on the production of high performance, consumer-oriented [[electric vehicle]]s. The firm was started in 2003 by engineers [[Martin Eberhard]] and Marc Tarpenning in [[San Carlos, California|San Carlos]], and has since grown to include several team members with extensive computer, electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds from around the world. The compay is named after the great Serbian American inventor [[Nikola Tesla]].

Initial investment came from [[PayPal]] co-founder [[Elon Musk]], who became Tesla's Chairman of the Board, leading Tesla's first two rounds and co-leading the third together with VantagePoint Venture Partners. Tesla's third round also included investment from prominent investors, such as [[Google]] co-founders [[Sergey Brin]] and [[Larry Page]], and former [[eBay]] president, [[Jeff Skoll]].

Tesla has a signifigant relationship with [[Lotus Cars|Lotus]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''', then codenamed ''Dark Star'', which Lotus's design studio won. Lotus will assemble the roadster, which is similar in size to their [[Lotus Elise|Elise]]. Tesla is currently working on a sedan, codenamed ''"White Star"'' which may be introduced as early as 2008.

Tesla plans to offer solar-photoelectric systems that will power the car from the car port at home allowing 80km of travel (further than the average driver travels daily) without even burdening the power grid, making the cars "energy positive".

'''Tesla Motors, Inc.''' is a [[Silicon Valley]] [[automobile]] startup company focusing on the production of high performance, consumer-oriented [[electric vehicle]]s. The firm was started in 2003 by [[engineer]]s [[Martin Eberhard]] and [[Marc Tarpenning]] in [[San Carlos, California|San Carlos]], and has since grown to include several team members with extensive [[computer]], electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds from around the world. The company is named after the great Serbian American inventor [[Nikola Tesla]].

Initial investment came from [[PayPal]] co-founder [[Elon Musk]], who became Tesla's Chairman of the Board, leading Tesla's first two rounds and co-leading the third together with VantagePoint Venture Partners. Tesla's third round also included investment from prominent investors, such as [[Google]] co-founders [[Sergey Brin]] and [[Larry Page]], and former [[eBay]] president, [[Jeff Skoll]].

Tesla has a signifigant relationship with [[Lotus Cars|Lotus]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''', then codenamed ''Dark Star'', which Lotus's design studio won. Lotus will assemble the roadster, which based around the underpinnings of their [[Lotus Elise|Elise]]. Tesla is currently working on a [[sedan]], codenamed ''"White Star"'' which may be introduced as early as [[2008]].

Tesla Motors' first production vehicle, The Tesla Roadster, is a performance sports car that has a range of 250 miles (400 kilometers), can accelerate from 0 to 60 MPH in 4 seconds and has a top speed of 130 MPH. Tesla plans to offer solar-[[photoelectric]] systems that will power the car without even burdening the [[power grid]], making the cars "energy positive".

'''Tesla Motors, Inc.''' is a [[Silicon Valley]] [[automobile]] startup company focusing on the production of high performance, consumer-oriented [[electric vehicle]]s. The firm was started in 2003 by [[engineer]]s [[Martin Eberhard]] and [[Marc Tarpenning]] in [[San Carlos, California|San Carlos]], and has since grown to include several team members with extensive [[computer]], electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds from around the world. The company is named after the great Serbian American inventor [[Nikola Tesla]].

== Financing ==

Initial investment came from [[PayPal]] co-founder [[Elon Musk]], who became Tesla's Chairman of the Board, leading Tesla's first two rounds and co-leading the third together with VantagePoint Venture Partners. Tesla's third round also included investment from prominent investors, such as [[Google]] co-founders [[Sergey Brin]] and [[Larry Page]], and former [[eBay]] president, [[Jeff Skoll]].

== Planned models ==

Tesla Motors' first production vehicle, the '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''', will be a performance sports car.  According to the company, the cars will have a range of 250 miles (400 kilometers), acceleration from 0 to 60 MPH in 4 seconds, and a top speed of 130 MPH.  Prototypes were introduced to the public in [[July 2006]], and the first production models are planned for summer [[2007]].  Demand was high for the first 'Signature One Hundred' Roadsters, which sold out in less than 3 weeks.

Tesla plans to offer solar-[[photoelectric]] systems that will offset power used by the home charger, allowing 50 miles (80 kilometers) of travel without burdening the [[power grid]], and thus making the package "energy positive" for a driver whose average daily mileage is less than that.

Tesla is also currently working on an unannounced [[sedan]], codenamed ''"White Star"'', which may be introduced as early as [[2008]].

== Partners ==

Tesla has a significant relationship with [[Lotus Cars|Lotus]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the Tesla Roadster, then codenamed ''Dark Star'', which Lotus's design studio won. The roadster is based on a modified [[Lotus Elise]] chassis, and includes other components from the Elise. Lotus will also assemble the car in England.

The development and production of this unnamed model will be funded by profits from the White Star sedan.http://www.teslamotors.com/blog1/?p=8 == Facilities == ===Headquaters=== Tesla Motors' headquarters are located in [[San Carlos, California]]. Much of the development of the [[Tesla Roadster]] occured here. ===United Kingdom=== Tesla Motors has facilities in [[England]] related to the assembly of the Tesla Roadster. ===Taiwan=== Tesla Motors has facilities in [[Taiwan]] for motor production. These will be used in the [[Tesla Roadster]] and the [[Tesla White Star]]. ===Michigan Technical Center=== The Michigan Technical Center, located in [[Rochester Hills, Michigan]], opened January 26, 2007. It is a 19,240 square-foot facility located at 1840 Enterprise Drive. The primary focus of the facility is on research & development for future Tesla products. The first priority is the four-door electric sports sedan project codenamed “[[Tesla White Star|WhiteStar]]”. It will be a four door, five-passenger, lightweight, high-performance sedan planned for production around 2009. The unnamed affordable third model will also be developed here. The region has an existing base of automotive companies, facilities and engineering talent, with thousands of highly experienced automotive experts. Utilizing these existing resources will reduce costly investments for Tesla Motors. ===Tesla Motors Assembly Facility (Albuquerque)=== Tesla Motors is constructing a new plant for mass production of its next vehicles, currently known as the [[Tesla White Star]]. The company announced plans to build the facility in [[Albuquerque, New Mexico]]. The facility is in [[Cordera Mesa]], [[Bernalillo County]], adjacent to a new Tempur-Pedic plant on the west side of the city, Over 400 people will work at the assembly facility. A total capital investment of $35 million is projected to be be spent on the 150,000 square foot plant. Competition for the plant was intense, as several states including California and Arizona were vying for the investment. SunCal has pledged to provide up to 75 acres of land next to the initial site at no cost, if Tesla Motors undertakes a major expansion in the future. SunCal recently acquired approximately 57,000 acres on Albuquerque’s west side, Construction will begin in April 2007, with completion expected by 2009, including tooling. Tesla Motors has stated that it intends to offer the [[Tesla White Star]] for sale in the Fall of 2009. This facility will be the world's first factory dedicated to the mass production of electric vehicles. Tesla Motors expects to initially build several thousand sedans a year but may later expand.[http://www.kobtv.com/index.cfm?viewer=storyviewer&id=30511&cat=NMTOPSTORIES][http://teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=257] The company intends to produce at least 10,000 [[Tesla White Star]]s per year. == Partners == Tesla has a significant relationship with [[Lotus Cars|Lotus]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the Tesla Roadster, then codenamed ''Dark Star'', which Lotus's design studio won. The roadster is based on a modified [[Lotus Elise]] chassis, and includes other components from the Elise. Lotus will also assemble the car in England. {{Infobox Company | company_name = Tesla Motors, Inc. | company_logo = [[Image:Teslalogo.PNG|center|170px]] | company_type = [[startup company|Startup]] | foundation = [[2003]] | location = [[San Carlos, California]] | key_people = [[Martin Eberhard]] ([[chief executive officer|CEO]])
[[Elon Musk]] ([[Chairman of the Board]]) | industry = [[Automobile|Automotive]] | products = [[Tesla Roadster]] | revenue = | operating_income = | net_income = | num_employees = 160 + | parent = | subsid = | homepage = http://www.teslamotors.com | footnotes = Motto: "Burn rubber, not gasoline" }} '''Tesla Motors, Inc.''' is a [[Silicon Valley]] [[automobile]] startup company focusing on the production of high performance, consumer-oriented [[electric vehicle]]s. The firm was started in 2003 by [[engineer]]s [[Martin Eberhard]] and [[Marc Tarpenning]] in [[San Carlos, California|San Carlos]], and has since grown to include several team members with extensive [[computer]], electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds from around the world. == Finance == Initial investment came from [[PayPal]] co-founder [[Elon Musk]], who became Tesla's Chairman of the Board, leading Tesla's first two rounds and co-leading the third together with [[VantagePoint Venture Partners]]. Tesla's third round also included investment from prominent entrepreneurs, such as [[Google]] co-founders [[Sergey Brin]] and [[Larry Page]], and former [[eBay]] president [[Jeff Skoll]]. == Planned models == Tesla Motors' first production vehicle, the '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''', will be a performance sports car. According to the company, the cars will have a range of 250 miles (400 kilometers), acceleration from 0 to 60 MPH (100 KM/H) in 3.9 seconds, and a top speed of 130 MPH (210 KM/H). Prototypes were introduced to the public in [[July 2006]], and the first production models are planned for fall [[2007]]. Demand was high for the first 'Signature One Hundred' Roadsters, which sold out in less than three weeks.[http://www.megawattmotorworks.com/display.asp?dismode=article&artid=293] According to a note on the company's web site, as of November 2006 the full run of the "2007 1/2" model have been sold out, constituting 220+ cars. As of March 15 2007, over '''350''' [[Tesla Roadster]]s have been reserved.{{cite news | publisher = ZDnet | date = 2007-03-15 | url = http://news.zdnet.com/2100-9595_22-6167658.html | format = [[HTML]] | accessdate = 2007-03-16 | title=Tesla to open five dealer outlets | language=English }} Tesla plans to offer home roof mounted solar-[[photovoltaic]] systems through [http://www.solarcity.com/ Solar City] that will offset power used by the home charger, allowing 50 miles (80 kilometers) of travel per day without burdening the [[power grid]], thus making the package "energy positive" for a driver whose average daily mileage is less than that. Tesla is also currently working on an unannounced [[sedan]], codenamed ''"[[Tesla White Star|White Star]]"'', which may be introduced in [[2009]] as a [[2010]] model. It is being designed as an alternative to the [[BMW]] [[BMW 5 Series|5 Series]], with an estimated price of $50,000-70,000. [http://news.windingroad.com/auto-news/tesla-motors-planning-electrifying-new-sedan/] White Star is to be built in a new plant in [[New Mexico]]. Future plans include a more affordable third model. The development and production of this unnamed model will be funded by profits from the White Star sedan.http://www.teslamotors.com/blog1/?p=8 == Facilities == ===Headquaters=== Tesla Motors' headquarters are located in [[San Carlos, California]]. Much of the development of the [[Tesla Roadster]] occured here. ===United Kingdom=== Tesla Motors has facilities in [[England]] related to the assembly of the Tesla Roadster. The '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''' is currently being manufactured by [[Lotus Cars]] in Hethel, England. ===Taiwan=== Tesla Motors has facilities in [[Taiwan]] for motor production. These will be used in the [[Tesla Roadster]] and the [[Tesla White Star]]. ===Michigan Technical Center=== The Michigan Technical Center, located in [[Rochester Hills, Michigan]], opened January 26, 2007. It is a 19,240 square-foot facility located at 1840 Enterprise Drive. Approximately $47.7 million will be spent on tooling. The primary focus of the facility is on research & development for future Tesla products. The first priority is the four-door electric sports sedan project codenamed “[[Tesla White Star|WhiteStar]]”. It will be a four door, five-passenger, lightweight, high-performance sedan planned for production around 2009. The unnamed affordable third model will also be developed here. The region has an existing base of automotive companies, facilities and engineering talent, with thousands of highly experienced automotive experts. Utilizing these existing resources will reduce costly investments for Tesla Motors. ===Tesla Motors Assembly Facility (Albuquerque)=== Tesla Motors is constructing a new plant for mass production of its next vehicles, currently known as the [[Tesla White Star]]. The company announced plans to build the facility in [[Albuquerque, New Mexico]]. The facility is in [[Cordera Mesa]], [[Bernalillo County]], adjacent to a new Tempur-Pedic plant on the west side of the city, Over 400 people will work at the assembly facility. A total capital investment of $35 million is projected to be be spent on the 150,000 square foot plant. Competition for the plant was intense, as several states including California, Arizona, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Michigan were vying for the investment. [[SunCal]], a land development company, has pledged to provide up to 75 acres of land next to the initial site at no cost, if Tesla Motors undertakes a major expansion in the future. SunCal recently acquired approximately 57,000 acres on Albuquerque’s west side. Construction will begin in April 2007, with completion expected by 2009, including tooling. Tesla Motors has stated that it intends to offer the [[Tesla White Star]] for sale in the Fall of 2009. This facility will be the world's first factory dedicated to the mass production of electric vehicles. Tesla Motors expects to initially build several thousand sedans a year but may later expand.[http://www.kobtv.com/index.cfm?viewer=storyviewer&id=30511&cat=NMTOPSTORIES][http://teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=257] The company intends to produce at least 10,000 [[Tesla White Star]]s per year. ==Service Centers== Service Centers for the [[Tesla Roadster]] will be located in the following United States Metropolitan Areas: *Los Angeles, California *San Francisco, California *New York, New York *Miami, Florida *Chicago, Illinois Other locations will be added over time. == Partners == Tesla has a significant relationship with [[Lotus Cars|Lotus]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the Tesla Roadster, then codenamed ''Dark Star'', which Lotus's design studio won. The roadster is based on a modified [[Lotus Elise]] chassis, and includes other components from the Elise. Lotus will also assemble the car in England. Tesla Motors has also licensed key technologies from [[AC Propulsion]], including a modified drivetrain. == Moniker == Tesla Motors is named after [[Nikola Tesla]], a [[Serbian]]-[[United States|American]] [[inventor]], [[electrical engineer]] and [[scientist]]. The principal visionary and inventor of alternating current, Tesla's designs and ideas changed the world, providing a stable means of delivering electricity over enormous distances; his battle with [[Thomas Edison|Edison]] over this issue went on for years with Tesla and [[George Westinghouse|Westinghouse]] ultimately triumphing. The [[Tesla Roadster]]'s powerplant is basically a Tesla [[three-phase]] [[electric motor]]. ==Board of Directors== *[[Elon Musk]] - Chairman of the board of directors, former President of [[Paypal]], founder and CEO of SpaceX *[[Martin Eberhard]] - Co-founder and CEO of Tesla Motors *[[Jim Marver]] - Co-founder and managing partner, VantagePoint Venture Partners *[[Kimbal Musk]] - CEO of Medium, Inc. *[[Simon Rothman]] - Former global vice president and general manager for eBay Motors *[[Steve Westly]] - Former Controller for California *[[Laurie Yoler]] - Principal, GrowthPoint Technology Partners == See also == *[[Tesla Roadster]] ==References== ==External links== * [http://www.teslamotors.com/ Official Tesla Motors website] * [http://www.wired.com/news/wiredmag/0,71414-0.html?tw=wn_index_1 Wired: ''Battery-Fueled Car Will Smoke You''] * [http://www.mercurynews.com/mld/mercurynews/news/breaking_news/14894457.htm ''Silicon Valley entrepreneurs race for electric car market''] ([[Associated Press|AP]]) * [http://www.channel4.com/4car/news/news-story.jsp?news_id=14656 Channel 4 news story] (contains speculative material and rumors) * [http://www.guardian.co.uk/cars/story/0,,1855488,00.html The Guardian newspaper on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_motors_ev.php Tesla Motors: Affordable Electric Cars are Coming] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster.php Tesla Roadster: The Electric Car that Redefines "Power" (Part 1)] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster2.php Tesla Roadster: New Power to the People (Part 2)] * [http://www.bbc.co.uk/radio4/news/inbusiness/inbusiness_20060921.shtml Plug in Cars] In Business, Radio 4, September 2006 * [http://www.teslamotorsclub.com Tesla Motors Club] Public forum oriented toward Tesla Roadster and general EV discussion * [http://www.greenvehiclenews.com/content/view/400/28/ Green Vehicle News on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.latimes.com/classified/automotive/highway1/la-hy-neil29nov29,0,7930203.story?coll=la-home-headlines Tesla Roadster review] by L.A. [[SunCal]], a land development company, has pledged to provide up to 75 acres (300,000 m²) of land next to the initial site at no cost, if Tesla Motors undertakes a major expansion in the future. SunCal recently acquired approximately 57,000 acres (230 km²) acres on Albuquerque’s west side. Construction will begin in April 2007, with completion expected by 2009, including tooling. Tesla Motors has stated that it intends to offer the [[Tesla White Star]] for sale in the Fall of 2009. This facility will be the world's first factory dedicated to the mass production of electric vehicles. Tesla Motors expects to initially build several thousand sedans a year but may later expand.[http://www.kobtv.com/index.cfm?viewer=storyviewer&id=30511&cat=NMTOPSTORIES][http://teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=257] The company intends to produce at least 10,000 [[Tesla White Star]]s per year. ==Service Centers== Service Centers for the Tesla Roadster are planned for the following United States Metropolitan Areas: *[[Los Angeles, California]] *[[San Francisco, California]] *[[New York, New York]] *[[Miami, Florida]] *[[Chicago, Illinois]] There are currently no planned service center locations outside of the United States. There is minimal maintenance required of an electric vehicle. There are no oil changes and brake maintenance is minor due to [[regenerative braking]]. Tesla Motors will build additional service centers over the next few years in order to support sales of its next vehicle, the sports sedan currently codename the [[Tesla White Star]]. "To do 10,000 units for Whitestar, we need to be in a lot more places," said [[Darryl Siry]], Vice president of Marketing. ==Partners== Tesla uses several domestic and overseas suppliers and partners. ===AC Propulsion=== Tesla Motors has licensed key technologies from [[AC Propulsion]], including a modified drivetrain. ===Lotus Cars=== Tesla has a significant relationship with [[Lotus Cars]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the [[Tesla Roadster]], then codenamed ''Dark Star'', which Lotus's design studio won. The roadster is based on a modified [[Lotus Elise]] chassis, and includes other components from the Elise. Final assembly of the Tesla Roadster is done by [[Lotus Cars]] in Hethel, England. ===Germany=== The brakes and airbags of the Tesla Roadster are made in Germany ===Norway=== The chassis of the Tesla Roadster is made in Norway ===Thailand=== The batteries are assembled into sheets for integration into the Energy Storage System by Tesla in [[Thailand]].http://www.forbes.com/technology/2007/03/18/tesla-electric-car-tech-cz_ec_0319valleyletter.html?partner=rss == Moniker == Tesla Motors is named after [[Nikola Tesla]], a [[Serbs|Serbian]]-[[United States|American]] [[inventor]], [[electrical engineer]] and [[scientist]]. The principal visionary and inventor of alternating current, Tesla's designs and ideas changed the world, providing a stable means of delivering electricity over enormous distances; his battle with [[Thomas Edison|Edison]] over this issue went on for years with Tesla and [[George Westinghouse|Westinghouse]] ultimately triumphing. The [[Tesla Roadster]]'s powerplant is basically a Tesla [[three-phase]] [[electric motor]]. ==Board of Directors== *[[Elon Musk]] - Chairman of the board of directors, former President of [[Paypal]], founder and CEO of SpaceX *[[Martin Eberhard]] - Co-founder and CEO of Tesla Motors *[[Jim Marver]] - Co-founder and managing partner, VantagePoint Venture Partners *[[Kimbal Musk]] - CEO of Medium, Inc. *[[Simon Rothman]] - Former global vice president and general manager for eBay Motors *[[Steve Westly]] - {{Infobox Company | company_name = Tesla Motors, Inc. | company_logo = [[Image:Teslalogo.PNG|center|170px]] | company_type = [[startup company|Startup]] | foundation = [[2003]] | location = [[San Carlos, California]] | key_people = [[Martin Eberhard]] ([[chief executive officer|CEO]])
[[Elon Musk]] ([[Chairman of the Board]]) | industry = [[Automobile|Automotive]] | products = [[Tesla Roadster]] | revenue = | operating_income = | net_income = | num_employees = 170 + | parent = | subsid = | homepage = http://www.teslamotors.com | footnotes = Motto: "Burn rubber, not gasoline" }} '''Tesla Motors, Inc.''' is a [[Silicon Valley]] [[automobile]] startup company focusing on the production of high performance, consumer-oriented [[electric vehicle]]s. The firm was started in 2003 by [[engineer]]s [[Martin Eberhard]] and [[Marc Tarpenning]] in [[San Carlos, California|San Carlos]], and has since grown to include several team members with extensive [[computer]], electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds from around the world. == Financing == Initial investment came from [[PayPal]] co-founder [[Elon Musk]], who became Tesla's Chairman of the Board. He also led Tesla Motors' first two rounds of financing and co-led the third round along with [[VantagePoint Venture Partners]]. Tesla's third round included investment from prominent entrepreneurs, such as [[Google]] co-founders [[Sergey Brin]] and [[Larry Page]], and former [[eBay]] President [[Jeff Skoll]]. As of March 2007, the company has raised over $60 million USD through private financing. Elon Musk, who was President of [[PayPal]] before being bought by [[eBay]], has contributed $30 million of his own money into the company. == Planned models == Tesla Motors' first production vehicle, the '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''', is a performance sports car. According to the company, the car has a range of 250 miles (400 km), acceleration from 0 to 60 mph (100 km/h) in 3.9 seconds, and a top speed of 130 mph (210 km/h). Prototypes were introduced to the public in [[July 2006]], and the first production models are planned for fall [[2007]]. Demand was high for the first 'Signature One Hundred' Roadsters, which sold out in less than three weeks.[http://www.megawattmotorworks.com/display.asp?dismode=article&artid=293] According to a note on the company's web site, as of November 2006 the full run of the "2007 1/2" model have been sold out, constituting 220+ cars. As of March 15 2007, over '''350''' [[Tesla Roadster]]s have been reserved.{{cite news | publisher = ZDnet | date = 2007-03-15 | url = http://news.zdnet.com/2100-9595_22-6167658.html | format = [[HTML]] | accessdate = 2007-03-16 | title=Tesla to open five dealer outlets | language=English }} Tesla plans to offer home roof mounted solar-[[photovoltaic]] systems through [http://www.solarcity.com/ Solar City] that will offset power used by the home charger, allowing 50 miles (80 km) of travel per day without burdening the [[power grid]], thus making the package "energy positive" for a driver whose average daily mileage is less than that. Tesla is also currently working on an unannounced [[sedan]], codenamed ''"[[Tesla White Star|White Star]]"'', which may be introduced in [[2009]] as a [[2010]] model. It is being designed as an alternative to the [[BMW]] [[BMW 5 Series|5 Series]], with an estimated price of $50,000-70,000. [http://news.windingroad.com/auto-news/tesla-motors-planning-electrifying-new-sedan/] White Star is to be built in a new plant in [[New Mexico]]. Future plans include a more affordable third model. The development and production of this unnamed model will be funded by profits from the White Star sedan.http://www.teslamotors.com/blog1/?p=8 == Facilities == ===Headquaters=== Tesla Motors' headquarters are located in [[San Carlos, California]]. Much of the development of the [[Tesla Roadster]] occurred here. ===United Kingdom=== Tesla Motors has facilities in [[England]] related to the assembly of the Tesla Roadster. The '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''' is currently being assembled by [[Lotus Cars]] in Hethel, England. ===Taiwan=== Tesla Motors has facilities in [[Taiwan]] for motor production. These will be used in the [[Tesla Roadster]] and the [[Tesla White Star]]. ===Michigan Technical Center=== The Michigan Technical Center, located in [[Rochester Hills, Michigan]], opened January 26, 2007. It is a 19,240 square-foot facility located at 1840 Enterprise Drive. Approximately $47.7 million will be spent on tooling. The primary focus of the facility is on research & development for future Tesla products. The first priority is the four-door electric sports sedan project codenamed “[[Tesla White Star|WhiteStar]]”. It will be a four door, five-passenger, lightweight, high-performance sedan planned for production around 2009. The unnamed affordable third model will also be developed here. The region has an existing base of automotive companies, facilities and engineering talent, with thousands of highly experienced automotive experts. Utilizing these existing resources will reduce costly investments for Tesla Motors. ===Tesla Motors Assembly Facility (Albuquerque)=== Tesla Motors is constructing a new plant for mass production of its next vehicles, currently known as the [[Tesla White Star]]. The company announced plans to build the facility in [[Albuquerque, New Mexico]]. The facility is in [[Cordera Mesa]], [[Bernalillo County]], adjacent to a new Tempur-Pedic plant on the west side of the city, Over 400 people will work at the assembly facility. A total capital investment of $35 million is projected to be be spent on the 150,000 square foot plant. Competition for the plant was intense, as several states including California, Arizona, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Michigan were vying for the investment. [[SunCal]], a land development company, has pledged to provide up to 75 acres (300,000 m²) of land next to the initial site at no cost, if Tesla Motors undertakes a major expansion in the future. SunCal recently acquired approximately 57,000 acres (230 km²) acres on Albuquerque’s west side. Construction will begin in April 2007, with completion expected by 2009, including tooling. Tesla Motors has stated that it intends to offer the [[Tesla White Star]] for sale in the Fall of 2009. This facility will be the world's first factory dedicated to the mass production of electric vehicles. Tesla Motors expects to initially build several thousand sedans a year but may later expand.[http://www.kobtv.com/index.cfm?viewer=storyviewer&id=30511&cat=NMTOPSTORIES][http://teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=257] The company intends to produce at least 10,000 [[Tesla White Star]]s per year. ==Service Centers== Service Centers for the Tesla Roadster are planned for the following United States Metropolitan Areas: *[[Los Angeles, California]] *[[San Francisco, California]] *[[New York, New York]] *[[Miami, Florida]] *[[Chicago, Illinois]] There are currently no planned service center locations outside of the United States. There is minimal maintenance required of an electric vehicle. There are no oil changes and brake maintenance is minor due to [[regenerative braking]]. Tesla Motors will build additional service centers over the next few years in order to support sales of its next vehicle, the sports sedan currently codename the [[Tesla White Star]]. "To do 10,000 units for Whitestar, we need to be in a lot more places," said [[Darryl Siry]], Vice president of Marketing. ==Partners== Tesla uses several domestic and overseas suppliers and partners. ===AC Propulsion=== [[Tesla Motors]] licensed [[AC Propulsion]]'s Reductive Charging(tm) patent, which integrates the charging electronics into the inverter in a way that reduces mass and complexity. [[Tesla Motors]] has designed and builds its own power electronics, motor, and other drivetrain components that incorporate this licensed technology from [[AC Propulsion]]. ===Lotus Cars=== Tesla has a significant relationship with [[Lotus Cars]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the [[Tesla Roadster]], then codenamed ''Dark Star'', which Lotus's design studio won. The roadster is based on a modified [[Lotus Elise]] chassis, and includes other components from the Elise. Final assembly of the Tesla Roadster is done by [[Lotus Cars]] in Hethel, England. ===Germany=== The brakes and airbags of the Tesla Roadster are made in Germany ===Norway=== The chassis of the Tesla Roadster is made in Norway ===Thailand=== The batteries are assembled into sheets for integration into the Energy Storage System by Tesla in [[Thailand]].http://www.forbes.com/technology/2007/03/18/tesla-electric-car-tech-cz_ec_0319valleyletter.html?partner=rss == Moniker == Tesla Motors is named after [[Nikola Tesla]], a [[Serbs|Serbian]]-[[United States|American]] [[inventor]], [[electrical engineer]] and [[scientist]]. The principal visionary and inventor of alternating current, Tesla's designs and ideas changed the world, providing a stable means of delivering electricity over enormous distances; his battle with [[Thomas Edison|Edison]] over this issue went on for years with Tesla and [[George Westinghouse|Westinghouse]] ultimately triumphing. The [[Tesla Roadster]]'s powerplant is basically a Tesla [[three-phase]] [[electric motor]]. ==Board of Directors== *[[Elon Musk]] - Chairman of the board of directors, former President of [[Paypal]], founder and CEO of SpaceX *[[Martin Eberhard]] - Co-founder and CEO of Tesla Motors *[[Jim Marver]] - Co-founder and managing partner, VantagePoint Venture Partners *[[Kimbal Musk]] - CEO of Medium, Inc. *[[Simon Rothman]] - Former global vice president and general manager for eBay Motors *[[Steve Westly]] - Former Controller for California *[[Laurie Yoler]] - Principal, GrowthPoint Technology Partners == See also == *[[Tesla Roadster]] ==References== ==External links== * [http://www.teslamotors.com/ Official Tesla Motors website] * [http://www.mercurynews.com/mld/mercurynews/news/breaking_news/14894457.htm ''Silicon Valley entrepreneurs race for electric car market''] ([[Associated Press|AP]]) * [http://www.channel4.com/4car/news/news-story.jsp?news_id=14656 Channel 4 news story] (contains speculative material and rumors) * [http://www.guardian.co.uk/cars/story/0,,1855488,00.html The Guardian newspaper on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_motors_ev.php Tesla Motors: Affordable Electric Cars are Coming] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster.php Tesla Roadster: The Electric Car that Redefines "Power" (Part 1)] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster2.php Tesla Roadster: New Power to the People (Part 2)] * [http://www.bbc.co.uk/radio4/news/inbusiness/inbusiness_20060921.shtml Plug in Cars] In Business, Radio 4, September 2006 * [http://www.teslamotorsclub.com Tesla Motors Club] Public forum oriented toward Tesla Roadster and general EV discussion * [http://www.greenvehiclenews.com/content/view/400/28/ Green Vehicle News on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.latimes.com/classified/automotive/highway1/la-hy-neil29nov29,0,7930203.story?coll=la-home-headlines Tesla Roadster review] by L.A. [[Elon Musk]] ([[Chairman of the Board]]) | industry = [[Automobile|Automotive]] | products = [[Tesla Roadster]] | revenue = | operating_income = | net_income = | num_employees = 180 + | parent = | subsid = | homepage = http://www.teslamotors.com | footnotes = Motto: "Burn rubber, not gasoline" }} '''Tesla Motors, Inc.''' is a [[Silicon Valley]] [[automobile]] startup company focusing on the production of high performance, consumer-oriented [[electric vehicle]]s. The firm was started in 2003 by [[engineer]]s [[Martin Eberhard]] and [[Marc Tarpenning]] in [[San Carlos, California|San Carlos]], and has since grown to include several team members with extensive [[computer]], electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds from around the world. bitch == Planned models == ===Tesla Roadster=== Tesla Motors' first production vehicle, the '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''', is a performance sports car. According to the company, the car has a range of more than 200 miles (322 km), acceleration from 0 to 60 mph (100 km/h) in 3.9 seconds, and a top speed of 130 mph (210 km/h). Prototypes were introduced to the public in [[July 2006]], and the first production models are planned for fall 2007. Demand was high for the first "Signature One Hundred" set of fully equipped Roadsters, which sold out in less than three weeks http://www.megawattmotorworks.com/display.asp?dismode=article&artid=293, and the second hundred sold out by October. As of April 18 2007, over '''380''' [[Tesla Roadster]]s have been reserved.{{cite news | publisher = AutoblogGreen | date = 2007-04-18 | url = http://www.autobloggreen.com/2007/04/18/bad-news-from-tesla-original-range-target-wont-be-met/ | format = [[HTML]] | accessdate = 2007-04-18 | title=Bad news from Tesla: Original range target won't be met! | language=English }} Tesla plans to offer home roof mounted solar-[[photovoltaic]] systems through [http://www.solarcity.com/ Solar City] that will offset power used by the home charger, allowing 50 miles (80 km) of travel per day without burdening the [[power grid]], thus making the package "energy positive" for a driver whose average daily mileage is less than that. ===Sports sedan=== Tesla is also currently working on an unannounced [[sedan]], codenamed ''"[[Tesla WhiteStar|WhiteStar]]"'', which may be introduced in [[2009]] as a [[2010]] model. It is being designed as an alternative to the [[BMW]] [[BMW 5 Series|5 Series]], with an estimated price of $50,000-70,000. [http://news.windingroad.com/auto-news/tesla-motors-planning-electrifying-new-sedan/] WhiteStar is to be built in a new plant in [[New Mexico]]. ===Future models=== Future plans include a more affordable third model. The development and production of this unnamed model will be funded by profits from the WhiteStar sedan.http://www.teslamotors.com/blog1/?p=8 == Facilities == ===Headquaters=== Tesla Motors' headquarters are located in [[San Carlos, California]]. Much of the development of the [[Tesla Roadster]] occurred here. ===United Kingdom=== Tesla Motors has facilities in [[England]] related to the design and assembly of the Tesla Roadster. The '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''' is currently being assembled by [[Lotus Cars]] in Hethel, England. ===Taiwan=== Tesla Motors has facilities in [[Taiwan]] for motor production. These will be used in the [[Tesla Roadster]] and the [[Tesla WhiteStar]]. ===Michigan Technical Center=== The Michigan Technical Center, located in [[Rochester Hills, Michigan]], opened January 26, 2007. It is a 19,240 square-foot facility located at 1840 Enterprise Drive. Approximately $47.7 million will be spent on tooling. The primary focus of the facility is on research & development for future Tesla products. The first priority is the four-door electric sports sedan project codenamed “[[Tesla WhiteStar|WhiteStar]]”. It will be a four door, five-passenger, lightweight, high-performance sedan planned for production around 2009. The unnamed more-affordable third model will also be developed here. The region has an existing base of automotive companies, facilities and engineering talent, with thousands of highly experienced automotive experts. Utilizing these existing resources will reduce costly investments for Tesla Motors. ===Tesla Motors Assembly Facility (Albuquerque)=== Tesla Motors is constructing a new plant for mass production of its next vehicles, currently known as the [[Tesla WhiteStar]]. The company announced plans to build the facility in [[Albuquerque, New Mexico]]. The facility is in [[Cordera Mesa]], [[Bernalillo County]], adjacent to a new Tempur-Pedic plant on the west side of the city, Over 400 people will work at the assembly facility. A total capital investment of $35 million is projected to be be spent on the 150,000 square foot plant. Competition for the plant was intense, as several states including California, Arizona, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Michigan were vying for the investment. [[SunCal]], a land development company, has pledged to provide up to 75 acres (300,000 m²) of land next to the initial site at no cost, if Tesla Motors undertakes a major expansion in the future. SunCal recently acquired approximately 57,000 acres (230 km²) acres on Albuquerque’s west side. Construction will begin in April 2007, with completion expected by 2009, including tooling. Tesla Motors has stated that it intends to offer the [[Tesla WhiteStar]] for sale in the Fall of 2009. This facility will be the world's first factory dedicated to the mass production of electric vehicles. Tesla Motors expects to initially build several thousand sedans a year but may later expand.[http://www.kobtv.com/index.cfm?viewer=storyviewer&id=30511&cat=NMTOPSTORIES][http://teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=257] The company intends to produce at least 10,000 [[Tesla WhiteStar]]s per year. ==Service Centers== Service Centers for the Tesla Roadster are planned for the following United States Metropolitan Areas: *[[Los Angeles, California]] *[[San Francisco, California]] *[[New York, New York]] *[[Miami, Florida]] *[[Chicago, Illinois]] [[Tesla Motors]] has stated that it will build additional service centers over the next few years in order to support sales of its next vehicle, the sports sedan currently codenamed the [[Tesla WhiteStar]]. "To do 10,000 units for WhiteStar, we need to be in a lot more places," said [[Darryl Siry]], Vice president of Marketing. Planning is currently underway for an additional fifteen service centres in major metropolitan locations http://pogue.blogs.nytimes.com/2007/04/05/solving-the-car-propulsion-problem/. There are currently no planned service center locations outside of the United States. There is minimal maintenance required of an electric vehicle. There are no oil changes and brake maintenance is minor due to [[regenerative braking]]. Transmission fluid and coolant changes will be required as for gasoline-engine cars. ==Partners== Tesla uses several domestic and overseas suppliers and partners. ===AC Propulsion=== [[Tesla Motors]] licensed [[AC Propulsion]]'s Reductive Charging(tm) patent, which integrates the charging electronics into the inverter in a way that reduces mass and complexity. [[Tesla Motors]] has designed and builds its own power electronics, motor, and other drivetrain components that incorporate this licensed technology from [[AC Propulsion]]. ===Lotus Cars=== Tesla has a significant relationship with [[Lotus Cars]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the [[Tesla Roadster]], then codenamed ''Dark Star'', which Lotus's design studio won. The roadster is based on a modified [[Lotus Elise]] chassis, and includes other components from the Elise. Final assembly of the Tesla Roadster is done by [[Lotus Cars]] in Hethel, England. ===Germany=== The brakes and airbags of the Tesla Roadster are made in Germany ===Norway=== The chassis of the Tesla Roadster is made in Norway ===Thailand=== The batteries are assembled into sheets for integration into the Energy Storage System by a Tesla supplier in [[Thailand]].http://www.forbes.com/technology/2007/03/18/tesla-electric-car-tech-cz_ec_0319valleyletter.html?partner=rss == Moniker == Tesla Motors is named after [[Nikola Tesla]], a [[Serbs|Serbian]]-[[United States|American]] [[inventor]], [[electrical engineer]] and [[scientist]]. The principal visionary and inventor of alternating current, Tesla's designs and ideas changed the world, providing a stable means of delivering electricity over enormous distances; his battle with [[Thomas Edison|Edison]] over this issue went on for years with Tesla and [[George Westinghouse|Westinghouse]] ultimately triumphing. The [[Tesla Roadster]]'s powerplant is basically a Tesla [[three-phase]] [[electric motor]]. ==Board of Directors== *[[Elon Musk]] - Chairman of the board of directors, former President of [[Paypal]], founder and CEO of SpaceX *[[Martin Eberhard]] - Co-founder and CEO of Tesla Motors *[[Jim Marver]] - Co-founder and managing partner, VantagePoint Venture Partners *[[Kimbal Musk]] - CEO of Medium, Inc. *[[Simon Rothman]] - Former global vice president and general manager for eBay Motors *[[Steve Westly]] - Former Controller for California *[[Laurie Yoler]] - Principal, GrowthPoint Technology Partners == See also == *[[Tesla Roadster]] ==References== ==External links== * [http://www.teslamotors.com/ Official Tesla Motors website] * [http://www.mercurynews.com/mld/mercurynews/news/breaking_news/14894457.htm ''Silicon Valley entrepreneurs race for electric car market''] ([[Associated Press|AP]]) * [http://www.channel4.com/4car/news/news-story.jsp?news_id=14656 Channel 4 news story] (contains speculative material and rumors) * [http://www.guardian.co.uk/cars/story/0,,1855488,00.html The Guardian newspaper on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_motors_ev.php Tesla Motors: Affordable Electric Cars are Coming] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster.php Tesla Roadster: The Electric Car that Redefines "Power" (Part 1)] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster2.php Tesla Roadster: New Power to the People (Part 2)] * [http://www.bbc.co.uk/radio4/news/inbusiness/inbusiness_20060921.shtml Plug in Cars] In Business, Radio 4, September 2006 * [http://www.teslamotorsclub.com Tesla Motors Club] Public forum oriented toward Tesla Roadster and general EV discussion * [http://www.greenvehiclenews.com/content/view/400/28/ Green Vehicle News on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.latimes.com/classified/automotive/highway1/la-hy-neil29nov29,0,7930203.story?coll=la-home-headlines Tesla Roadster review] by L.A. Times' Pulitzer-winning auto critic Dan Neil * [http://usfmbapodcast.com/2007/01/28/13-ceo-of-tesla-motors-the-fastest-electric-car-on-earth/ Listen to a great presentation by Tesla's Founder & CEO] USF MBA Podcast, Jan 28th, 2007 * [http://www.kobtv.com/index.cfm?viewer=storyviewer&id=30511&cat=NMTOPSTORIES] [[Category:Companies based in San Mateo County]] [[Category:Companies established in 2003]] [[Category:Motor vehicle manufacturers of the United States]] [[Category:Car manufacturers]] [[de:Tesla Motors]] [[fr:Tesla Motors]] [[it:Tesla Motors]] [[lt:Tesla Motors]] [[nl:Tesla (automerk)]] [[no:Tesla Motors]] [[pl:Tesla Motors]] [[pt:Tesla Motors]] [[tr:Tesla motors]]

Tesla Motors expects to initially build several thousand sedans a year but may later expand.[http://www.kobtv.com/index.cfm?viewer=storyviewer&id=30511&cat=NMTOPSTORIES][http://teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=257] The company intends to produce at least 10,000 [[Tesla WhiteStar]]s per year. ==Service Centers== Service Centers for the Tesla Roadster are planned for the following United States Metropolitan Areas: *[[Los Angeles, California]] *[[San Francisco, California]] *[[New York, New York]] *[[Miami, Florida]] *[[Chicago, Illinois]] Tesla Motors has stated that it will build additional service centers over the next few years in order to support sales of its next vehicle, the sports sedan currently codenamed the [[Tesla WhiteStar]]. "To do 10,000 units for WhiteStar, we need to be in a lot more places," said [[Darryl Siry]], Vice president of Marketing. Planning is currently underway for an additional fifteen service centres in major metropolitan locations http://pogue.blogs.nytimes.com/2007/04/05/solving-the-car-propulsion-problem/. There are currently no planned service center locations outside of the United States. There is minimal maintenance required of an electric vehicle. There are no oil changes and brake maintenance is minor due to [[regenerative braking]]. Transmission fluid and coolant changes will be required as for gasoline-engine cars. ==Partners== Tesla uses several domestic and overseas suppliers and partners. ===AC Propulsion=== Tesla Motors licensed [[AC Propulsion]]'s Reductive Charging(tm) patent, which integrates the charging electronics into the inverter in a way that reduces mass and complexity. Tesla Motors has designed and builds its own power electronics, motor, and other drivetrain components that incorporate this licensed technology from [[AC Propulsion]]. ===Lotus Cars=== Tesla has a significant relationship with [[Lotus Cars]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the [[Tesla Roadster]], then codenamed ''Dark Star'', which Lotus's design studio won. The roadster is based on a modified [[Lotus Elise]] chassis, and includes other components from the Elise. Final assembly of the Tesla Roadster is done by [[Lotus Cars]] in Hethel, England. ===Germany=== The brakes and airbags of the Tesla Roadster are made in Germany ===Norway=== The chassis of the Tesla Roadster is made in Norway ===Thailand=== The batteries are assembled into sheets for integration into the Energy Storage System by a Tesla supplier in [[Thailand]].http://www.forbes.com/technology/2007/03/18/tesla-electric-car-tech-cz_ec_0319valleyletter.html?partner=rss == Moniker == Tesla Motors is named after [[Nikola Tesla]], a [[Serbs|Serbian]]-[[United States|American]] [[inventor]], [[electrical engineer]] and [[scientist]]. The principal visionary and inventor of alternating current, Tesla's designs and ideas changed the world, providing a stable means of delivering electricity over enormous distances; his battle with [[Thomas Edison|Edison]] over this issue went on for years with Tesla and [[George Westinghouse|Westinghouse]] ultimately triumphing. The [[Tesla Roadster]]'s powerplant is basically a Tesla [[three-phase]] [[electric motor]]. ==Board of Directors== *[[Elon Musk]] - Chairman of the board of directors, former President of [[Paypal]], founder and CEO of SpaceX *[[Martin Eberhard]] - Co-founder and CEO of Tesla Motors *[[Jim Marver]] - Co-founder and managing partner, VantagePoint Venture Partners *[[Kimbal Musk]] - CEO of Medium, Inc. *[[Simon Rothman]] - Former global vice president and general manager for eBay Motors *[[Steve Westly]] - Former Controller for California *[[Laurie Yoler]] - Principal, GrowthPoint Technology Partners == See also == *[[Tesla Roadster]] *[[Th!nk City]] ==References== ==External links== * [http://www.teslamotors.com/ Official Tesla Motors website] * [http://www.channel4.com/4car/news/news-story.jsp?news_id=14656 Channel 4 news story] (contains speculative material and rumors) * [http://www.guardian.co.uk/cars/story/0,,1855488,00.html The Guardian newspaper on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_motors_ev.php Tesla Motors: Affordable Electric Cars are Coming] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster.php Tesla Roadster: The Electric Car that Redefines "Power" (Part 1)] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster2.php Tesla Roadster: New Power to the People (Part 2)] * [http://www.bbc.co.uk/radio4/news/inbusiness/inbusiness_20060921.shtml Plug in Cars] In Business, Radio 4, September 2006 * [http://www.teslamotorsclub.com Tesla Motors Club] Public forum oriented toward Tesla Roadster and general EV discussion * [http://www.greenvehiclenews.com/content/view/400/28/ Green Vehicle News on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.latimes.com/classified/automotive/highway1/la-hy-neil29nov29,0,7930203.story?coll=la-home-headlines Tesla Roadster review] by L.A. {{Infobox Company | company_name = Tesla Motors, Inc. | company_logo = [[Image:Teslalogo.PNG|center|170px]] | company_type = [[Private company|Private]] | foundation = [[2003]] | location = [[San Carlos, California]] | key_people = [[Martin Eberhard]] ([[chief executive officer|CEO]])
[[Elon Musk]] ([[Chairman of the Board]]) | industry = [[Automobile|Automotive]] | products = [[Tesla Roadster]] | revenue = | operating_income = | net_income = | num_employees = 200 + | parent = | subsid = | homepage = http://www.teslamotors.com | slogan = "Burn rubber, not gasoline" }} '''Tesla Motors, Inc.''' is a [[Silicon Valley]] [[automobile]] startup company focusing on the production of high performance, consumer-oriented [[battery electric vehicle|electric vehicle]]s. The firm was started in the summer of 2003 by [[engineer]]s [[Martin Eberhard]] and [[Marc Tarpenning]] in [[San Carlos, California|San Carlos]], and has since grown to include several team members with extensive [[computer]], electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds from around the world. == Financing == Initial investment came from [[PayPal]] co-founder [[Elon Musk]], who became Tesla's Chairman of the Board. He also led Tesla Motors' first two rounds of financing and co-led the third round along with [[VantagePoint Venture Partners]]. Tesla's third round included investment from prominent entrepreneurs, such as [[Google]] co-founders [[Sergey Brin]] and [[Larry Page]], and former [[eBay]] President [[Jeff Skoll]]. As of May 2007, the company has raised over $105 million USD through private financing. Elon Musk, who was President of [[PayPal]] before it was bought by [[eBay]], has contributed $37 million of his own money into the company. == Planned models == ===Tesla Roadster=== Tesla Motors' first production vehicle, the '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''', is a performance sports car. According to the company, the car has a range of more than 200 miles (322 km), acceleration from 0 to 60 mph (100 km/h) in 3.9 seconds, and a top speed of 130 mph (210 km/h). Prototypes were introduced to the public in [[July 2006]], and the first production models (the 2008 model year), are planned for winter 2007. Demand was high for the first "Signature One Hundred" set of fully equipped Roadsters, which sold out in less than three weeks http://www.megawattmotorworks.com/display.asp?dismode=article&artid=293, and the second hundred sold out by October. As of May 11 2007, over '''400''' [[Tesla Roadster]]s have been reserved.{{cite news | publisher = Green Car Congress | date = 2007-05-11 | url = http://www.greencarcongress.com/2007/05/tesla_motors_se.html#more | format = [[HTML]] | accessdate = 2007-05-11 | title=Tesla Motors Secures $45 Million Series D Round | language=English }} Tesla plans to offer home roof mounted solar-[[photovoltaic]] systems through [http://www.solarcity.com/ Solar City] that will offset power used by the home charger, allowing 50 miles (80 km) of travel per day without burdening the [[power grid]], thus making the package "energy positive" for a driver whose average daily mileage is less than that. ===Sports sedan=== Tesla is also currently working on an announced but unrevealed [[sedan]], codenamed ''"[[Tesla WhiteStar|WhiteStar]]"'', which may be introduced in [[2009]] as a [[2010]] model. It is being designed as an alternative to the [[BMW]] [[BMW 5 Series|5 Series]], with an estimated price of $50,000-70,000. [http://news.windingroad.com/auto-news/tesla-motors-planning-electrifying-new-sedan/] WhiteStar is to be built in a new plant in [[New Mexico]]. ===Future models=== Future plans include a more affordable third model. The development and production of this unnamed model will be funded by profits from the WhiteStar sedan.http://www.teslamotors.com/blog1/?p=8 == Facilities == ===Headquarters=== Tesla Motors' headquarters are located in [[San Carlos, California]]. Much of the development of the [[Tesla Roadster]] occurred here. ===United Kingdom=== Tesla Motors has facilities in [[England]] related to the design and assembly of the Tesla Roadster. The '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''' is currently being assembled by [[Lotus Cars]] in Hethel, England. ===Taiwan=== Tesla Motors has facilities in [[Taiwan]] for motor production. These will be used in the [[Tesla Roadster]] and the [[Tesla WhiteStar]]. ===Michigan Technical Center=== The Michigan Technical Center, located in [[Rochester Hills, Michigan]], opened January 26, 2007. It is a 19,240 square-foot facility located at 1840 Enterprise Drive. Approximately $47.7 million will be spent on tooling. The primary focus of the facility is on research & development for future Tesla products. The first priority is the four-door electric sports sedan project codenamed “[[Tesla WhiteStar|WhiteStar]]”. It will be a four door, five-passenger, lightweight, high-performance sedan planned for production around 2009. The unnamed more-affordable third model will also be developed here. The region has an existing base of automotive companies, facilities and engineering talent, with thousands of highly experienced automotive experts. Utilizing these existing resources will reduce costly investments for Tesla Motors. ===Tesla Motors Assembly Facility (Albuquerque)=== Tesla Motors is constructing a new plant for mass production of its next vehicles, currently known as the [[Tesla WhiteStar]]. The company announced plans to build the facility in [[Albuquerque, New Mexico]]. The facility is in [[Cordera Mesa]], [[Bernalillo County]], adjacent to a new Tempur-Pedic plant on the west side of the city, Over 400 people will work at the assembly facility. A total capital investment of $35 million is projected to be be spent on the 150,000 square foot plant. Competition for the plant was intense, as several states including California, Arizona, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Michigan were vying for the investment. [[SunCal]], a land development company, has pledged to provide up to 75 acres (300,000 m²) of land next to the initial site at no cost, if Tesla Motors undertakes a major expansion in the future. SunCal recently acquired approximately 57,000 acres (230 km²) acres on Albuquerque’s west side. Construction will begin in Summer 2007, with completion expected by 2009, including tooling. Tesla Motors has stated that it intends to offer the [[Tesla WhiteStar]] for sale in the Fall of 2009. This facility will be the world's first factory dedicated to the mass production of electric vehicles. Tesla Motors expects to initially build several thousand sedans a year but may later expand.[http://www.kobtv.com/index.cfm?viewer=storyviewer&id=30511&cat=NMTOPSTORIES][http://teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=257] The company intends to produce at least 10,000 [[Tesla WhiteStar]]s per year. ==Service Centers== Service Centers for the Tesla Roadster are planned for the following United States Metropolitan Areas: *[[Los Angeles, California]] *[[San Francisco, California]] *[[New York, New York]] *[[Miami, Florida]] *[[Chicago, Illinois]] Tesla Motors has stated that it will build additional service centers over the next few years in order to support sales of its next vehicle, the sports sedan currently codenamed the [[Tesla WhiteStar]]. "To do 10,000 units for WhiteStar, we need to be in a lot more places," said [[Darryl Siry]], Vice president of Marketing. Planning is currently underway for an additional fifteen service centres in major metropolitan locations http://pogue.blogs.nytimes.com/2007/04/05/solving-the-car-propulsion-problem/. There are currently no planned service center locations outside of the United States. There is minimal maintenance required of an electric vehicle. There are no oil changes and brake maintenance is minor due to [[regenerative braking]]. Transmission, brake, and cooling system fluid changes will be required as for gasoline-engine cars. ==Partners== Tesla uses several domestic and overseas suppliers and partners. ===AC Propulsion=== Tesla Motors licensed [[AC Propulsion]]'s Reductive Charging(tm) patent, which integrates the charging electronics into the inverter in a way that reduces mass and complexity. Tesla Motors has designed and builds its own power electronics, motor, and other drivetrain components that incorporate this licensed technology from [[AC Propulsion]]. ===Lotus Cars=== Tesla has a significant relationship with [[Lotus Cars]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the [[Tesla Roadster]], then codenamed ''Dark Star'', which Lotus's design studio won. The roadster is based on a modified [[Lotus Elise]] chassis, and includes other components from the Elise. Final assembly of the Tesla Roadster is done by [[Lotus Cars]] in Hethel, England. ===Germany=== The brakes and airbags of the Tesla Roadster are made in Germany. ===Norway=== The chassis of the Tesla Roadster is made in Norway. ===Thailand=== The batteries are assembled into sheets for integration into the Energy Storage System by a Tesla supplier in [[Thailand]].http://www.forbes.com/technology/2007/03/18/tesla-electric-car-tech-cz_ec_0319valleyletter.html?partner=rss == Moniker == Tesla Motors is named after [[Nikola Tesla]], a [[Serbs|Serbian]]-[[United States|American]] [[inventor]], [[electrical engineer]] and [[scientist]]. The principal visionary and inventor of alternating current, Tesla's designs and ideas changed the world, providing a stable means of delivering electricity over enormous distances; his battle with [[Thomas Edison|Edison]] over this issue went on for years with Tesla and [[George Westinghouse|Westinghouse]] ultimately triumphing. The [[Tesla Roadster]]'s powerplant is basically a Tesla [[three-phase]] [[electric motor]]. ==Board of Directors== *[[Elon Musk]] - Chairman of the board of directors, former President of [[Paypal]], founder and CEO of SpaceX *[[Martin Eberhard]] - Co-founder and CEO of Tesla Motors *[[Jim Marver]] - Co-founder and managing partner, VantagePoint Venture Partners *[[Kimbal Musk]] - CEO of Medium, Inc. *[[Simon Rothman]] - Former global vice president and general manager for eBay Motors *[[Steve Westly]] - Former Controller for California *[[Laurie Yoler]] - Principal, GrowthPoint Technology Partners == See also == *[[Tesla Roadster]] *[[Th!nk City]] *[[La Jamais Contente]] the ancestor of the Tesla Roadster. ==References== ==External links== * [http://www.teslamotors.com/ Official Tesla Motors website] * [http://www.channel4.com/4car/news/news-story.jsp?news_id=14656 Channel 4 news story] (contains speculative material and rumors) * [http://www.guardian.co.uk/cars/story/0,,1855488,00.html The Guardian newspaper on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_motors_ev.php Tesla Motors: Affordable Electric Cars are Coming] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster.php Tesla Roadster: The Electric Car that Redefines "Power" (Part 1)] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster2.php Tesla Roadster: New Power to the People (Part 2)] * [http://www.bbc.co.uk/radio4/news/inbusiness/inbusiness_20060921.shtml Plug in Cars] In Business, Radio 4, September 2006 * [http://www.teslamotorsclub.com Tesla Motors Club] Public forum oriented toward Tesla Roadster and general EV discussion * [http://www.greenvehiclenews.com/content/view/400/28/ Green Vehicle News on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.latimes.com/classified/automotive/highway1/la-hy-neil29nov29,0,7930203.story?coll=la-home-headlines Tesla Roadster review] by L.A. [[Elon Musk]] ([[Chairman of the Board]]) | industry = [[Automobile|Automotive]] | products = [[Tesla Roadster]] | revenue = | operating_income = | net_income = | num_employees = 200 + | parent = | subsid = | homepage = http://www.teslamotors.com | slogan = "Burn rubber, not gasoline" }} '''Tesla Motors, Inc.''' is a [[Silicon Valley]] [[automobile]] startup company focusing on the production of high performance, consumer-oriented [[battery electric vehicle|electric vehicle]]s. The firm was started in the summer of 2003 by [[engineer]]s [[Martin Eberhard]] and [[Marc Tarpenning]] in [[San Carlos, California|San Carlos]], and has since grown to include several team members with extensive [[computer]], electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds from around the world. Tesla Motors is named after [[Serbs of Croatia|Croatian Serb]] [[inventor]], [[electrical engineer]] and [[scientist]] [[Nikola Tesla]]. == Financing == Initial investment came from [[PayPal]] co-founder [[Elon Musk]], who became Tesla's Chairman of the Board. He also led Tesla Motors' first two rounds of financing and co-led the third round along with [[VantagePoint Venture Partners]]. Tesla's third round included investment from prominent entrepreneurs, such as [[Google]] co-founders [[Sergey Brin]] and [[Larry Page]], and former [[eBay]] President [[Jeff Skoll]]. As of May 2007, the company has raised over $105 million USD through private financing. Elon Musk, who was President of [[PayPal]] before it was bought by [[eBay]], has contributed $37 million of his own money into the company. == Planned models == ===Tesla Roadster=== Tesla Motors' first production vehicle, the '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''', is a performance sports car. According to the company, the car has a range of more than 200 miles (322 km), acceleration from 0 to 60 mph (100 km/h) in 3.9 seconds, and a top speed of 130 mph (210 km/h) (limited for safety). Prototypes were introduced to the public in [[July 2006]], and the first production models (the 2008 model year), are planned for winter 2007. Demand was high for the first "Signature One Hundred" set of fully equipped Roadsters, which sold out in less than three weeks http://www.megawattmotorworks.com/display.asp?dismode=article&artid=293, and the second hundred sold out by October. As of May 11 2007, over '''400''' [[Tesla Roadster]]s have been reserved.{{cite news | publisher = Green Car Congress | date = 2007-05-11 | url = http://www.greencarcongress.com/2007/05/tesla_motors_se.html#more | format = [[HTML]] | accessdate = 2007-05-11 | title=Tesla Motors Secures $45 Million Series D Round | language=English }} Tesla plans to offer home roof mounted solar-[[photovoltaic]] systems through [http://www.solarcity.com/ Solar City] that will offset power used by the home charger, allowing 50 miles (80 km) of travel per day without burdening the [[power grid]], thus making the package "energy positive" for a driver whose average daily mileage is less than that. ===Sports sedan=== Tesla is also currently working on an announced but unrevealed [[sedan]], codenamed ''"[[Tesla WhiteStar|WhiteStar]]"'', which may be introduced in [[2009]] as a [[2010]] model. It is being designed as an alternative to the [[BMW]] [[BMW 5 Series|5 Series]], with an estimated price of $50,000-70,000. [http://news.windingroad.com/auto-news/tesla-motors-planning-electrifying-new-sedan/] WhiteStar is to be built in a new plant in [[New Mexico]]. ===Future models=== Future plans include a more affordable third model. The development and production of this unnamed model will be funded by profits from the WhiteStar sedan.http://www.teslamotors.com/blog1/?p=8 == Facilities == ===Headquarters=== Tesla Motors' headquarters are located in [[San Carlos, California]]. Much of the development of the [[Tesla Roadster]] occurred here. ===United Kingdom=== Tesla Motors has facilities in [[England]] related to the design and assembly of the Tesla Roadster. The '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''' is currently being assembled by [[Lotus Cars]] in Hethel, England. ===Taiwan=== Tesla Motors has facilities in [[Taiwan]] for motor production. These will be used in the [[Tesla Roadster]] and the [[Tesla WhiteStar]]. ===Michigan Technical Center=== The Michigan Technical Center, located in [[Rochester Hills, Michigan]], opened January 26, 2007. It is a 19,240 square-foot facility located at 1840 Enterprise Drive. Approximately $47.7 million will be spent on tooling. The primary focus of the facility is on research & development for future Tesla products. The first priority is the four-door electric sports sedan project codenamed “[[Tesla WhiteStar|WhiteStar]]”. It will be a four door, five-passenger, lightweight, high-performance sedan planned for production around 2009. The unnamed more-affordable third model will also be developed here. The region has an existing base of automotive companies, facilities and engineering talent, with thousands of highly experienced automotive experts. Utilizing these existing resources will reduce costly investments for Tesla Motors. ===Tesla Motors Assembly Facility (Albuquerque)=== Tesla Motors is constructing a new plant for mass production of its next vehicles, currently known as the [[Tesla WhiteStar]]. The company announced plans to build the facility in [[Albuquerque, New Mexico]]. The facility is in [[Cordera Mesa]], [[Bernalillo County]], adjacent to a new Tempur-Pedic plant on the west side of the city. Over 400 people will work at the assembly facility. A total capital investment of $35 million is projected to be spent on the 150,000 square foot plant. Competition for the plant was intense, as several states including California, Arizona, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Michigan were vying for the investment. [[SunCal]], a land development company, has pledged to provide up to 75 acres (300,000 m²) of land next to the initial site at no cost, if Tesla Motors undertakes a major expansion in the future. SunCal recently acquired approximately 57,000 acres (230 km²) acres on Albuquerque’s west side. Construction will begin in Summer 2007, with completion expected by 2009, including tooling. Tesla Motors has stated that it intends to offer the [[Tesla WhiteStar]] for sale in the Fall of 2009. This facility will be the world's first factory dedicated to the mass production of electric vehicles. Tesla Motors expects to initially build several thousand sedans a year but may later expand.[http://www.kobtv.com/index.cfm?viewer=storyviewer&id=30511&cat=NMTOPSTORIES][http://teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=257] The company intends to produce at least 10,000 [[Tesla WhiteStar]]s per year. ==Service Centers== Service Centers for the Tesla Roadster are planned for the following United States Metropolitan Areas: *[[Los Angeles, California]] *[[San Francisco, California]] *[[New York, New York]] *[[Miami, Florida]] *[[Chicago, Illinois]] Tesla Motors has stated that it will build additional service centers over the next few years in order to support sales of its next vehicle, the sports sedan currently codenamed the [[Tesla WhiteStar]]. "To do 10,000 units for WhiteStar, we need to be in a lot more places," said [[Darryl Siry]], Vice president of Marketing. Planning is currently underway for an additional fifteen service centres in major metropolitan locations http://pogue.blogs.nytimes.com/2007/04/05/solving-the-car-propulsion-problem/. There are currently no planned service center locations outside of the United States. There is minimal maintenance required of an electric vehicle. There are no oil changes and brake maintenance is minor due to [[regenerative braking]]. Transmission, brake, and cooling system fluid changes will be required as for gasoline-engine cars. ==Partners== Tesla uses several domestic and overseas suppliers and partners. ===AC Propulsion=== Tesla Motors licensed [[AC Propulsion]]'s Reductive Charging(tm) patent, which integrates the charging electronics into the inverter in a way that reduces mass and complexity. Tesla Motors has designed and builds its own power electronics, motor, and other drivetrain components that incorporate this licensed technology from [[AC Propulsion]]. ===Lotus Cars=== Tesla has a significant relationship with [[Lotus Cars]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the [[Tesla Roadster]], then codenamed ''Dark Star'', which Lotus's design studio won. The roadster is based on a modified [[Lotus Elise]] chassis, and includes other components from the Elise. Final assembly of the Tesla Roadster is done by [[Lotus Cars]] in Hethel, England. ===Germany=== The brakes and airbags of the Tesla Roadster are made in Germany. ===Norway=== The chassis of the Tesla Roadster is made in Norway. ===Thailand=== The batteries are assembled into sheets for integration into the Energy Storage System by a Tesla supplier in [[Thailand]].http://www.forbes.com/technology/2007/03/18/tesla-electric-car-tech-cz_ec_0319valleyletter.html?partner=rss == Moniker == Tesla Motors is named after [[Nikola Tesla]], a [[Serbs|Serbian]] [[inventor]], [[electrical engineer]] and [[scientist]]. The principal visionary and inventor of alternating current, Tesla's designs and ideas changed the world, providing a stable means of delivering electricity over enormous distances; his battle with [[Thomas Edison|Edison]] over this issue went on for years with Tesla and [[George Westinghouse]] ultimately triumphing. The [[Tesla Roadster]]'s powerplant is basically a Tesla [[three-phase]] [[electric motor]]. Tesla would have been the worlds first Trillionaire however he ripped up his contract when he learned that his commission for AC current would force Westinghouse into bankruptcy.{{Fact|date=July 2007}} Tesla died alone and in debt. ==Board of Directors== *[[Elon Musk]] - Chairman of the board of directors, former President of [[Paypal]], founder and CEO of SpaceX *[[Martin Eberhard]] - Co-founder and CEO of Tesla Motors *[[Jim Marver]] - Co-founder and managing partner, VantagePoint Venture Partners *[[Kimbal Musk]] - CEO of Medium, Inc. *[[Simon Rothman]] - Former global vice president and general manager for eBay Motors *[[Steve Westly]] - Former Controller for California *[[Laurie Yoler]] - Principal, GrowthPoint Technology Partners == See also == *[[Tesla Roadster]] *[[Think Nordic]] and [[Th!nk City]] *[[La Jamais Contente]] the ancestor of the Tesla Roadster. ==References== ==External links== * [http://www.teslamotors.com/ Official Tesla Motors website] * [http://www.channel4.com/4car/news/news-story.jsp?news_id=14656 Channel 4 news story] (contains speculative material and rumors) * [http://www.guardian.co.uk/cars/story/0,,1855488,00.html The Guardian newspaper on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_motors_ev.php Tesla Motors: Affordable Electric Cars are Coming] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster.php Tesla Roadster: The Electric Car that Redefines "Power" (Part 1)] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster2.php Tesla Roadster: New Power to the People (Part 2)] * [http://www.bbc.co.uk/radio4/news/inbusiness/inbusiness_20060921.shtml Plug in Cars] In Business, Radio 4, September 2006 * [http://www.teslamotorsclub.com Tesla Motors Club] Public forum oriented toward Tesla Roadster and general EV discussion * [http://www.greenvehiclenews.com/content/view/400/28/ Green Vehicle News on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.latimes.com/classified/automotive/highway1/la-hy-neil29nov29,0,7930203.story?coll=la-home-headlines Tesla Roadster review] by L.A. Times' Pulitzer-winning auto critic Dan Neil * [http://usfmbapodcast.com/2007/01/28/13-ceo-of-tesla-motors-the-fastest-electric-car-on-earth/ USF MBA Podcast, Jan 28th, 2007] Audio presentation by Tesla's Co-Founder & CEO [[Category:Companies based in San Mateo County]] [[Category:Companies established in 2003]] [[Category:Motor vehicle manufacturers of the United States]] [[Category:Car manufacturers]] [[de:Tesla Motors]] [[eo:Tesla Motors]] [[fr:Tesla Motors]] [[it:Tesla Motors]] [[lt:Tesla Motors]] [[nl:Tesla (automerk)]] [[no:Tesla Motors]] [[pl:Tesla Motors]] [[pt:Tesla Motors]] [[tr:Tesla motors]]
