[[Think Nordic|Think Global]] was to be the first customer, placing an order for development and delivery of battery packs for their electric cars. However, Tesla put their plans on hold due to delays in production and engineering, and Think has accordingly found a new battery supplier, [[EnerDel]]. {{cite press release | publisher = [[Tesla Motors]] | date = 2007-05-22 | url = http://www.teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=509 | format = [[HTML]] | title= Tesla Energy Group, a New Division of Tesla Motors, Signs Development and Supply Agreement Worth $43 Million with Think of Norway |language=English }} == Moniker == Tesla Motors is named after [[Nikola Tesla]], a [[Serbs|Serbian]] [[inventor]], [[electrical engineer]] and [[scientist]]. The principal visionary and inventor of [[alternating current]], Tesla's designs and ideas changed the world, providing a stable means of delivering electricity over enormous distances; the ''[[War of Currents]]'' with [[Thomas Edison|Edison]] over this issue went on for years with Tesla and [[George Westinghouse]] ultimately triumphing. The Tesla Roadster's power plant is basically a Tesla [[three-phase electric power|three-phase]] [[AC motor|AC induction motor]]. ==Board of Directors== * [[Elon Musk]] - Chairman of the board of directors, former President of [[Paypal]], founder and CEO of [[SpaceX]] * [[Ze'ev Drori]] - CEO of Tesla Motors * [[Ira Ehrenpreis]] - General Partner, [[Technology Partners]] * [[Antonio J. Gracias]] - CEO and Chairman of the Investment Committee at [[Valor Equity Partners]] * [[Jim Marver]] - Co-founder and managing partner, [[VantagePoint Venture Partners]] * [[Kimbal Musk]] - CEO of [[Medium, Inc.]] * [[Steve Westly]] - Former Controller for California and founder of [[The Westly Group]] * [[Laurie Yoler]] - Managing Director, [[GrowthPoint Technology Partners]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== * [http://www.teslamotors.com/ Official Tesla Motors website] * [http://edcorner.stanford.edu/authorMaterialInfo.html?mid=1862&author=253 Distribution plan for the new Electric Car], Martin Eberhard speaks at Stanford * [http://www.channel4.com/4car/news/news-story.jsp?news_id=14656 Channel 4 news story] (contains speculative material and rumors) * [http://www.guardian.co.uk/cars/story/0,,1855488,00.html The Guardian newspaper on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_motors_ev.php Tesla Motors: Affordable Electric Cars are Coming] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster.php Tesla Roadster: The Electric Car that Redefines "Power" (Part 1)] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster2.php Tesla Roadster: New Power to the People (Part 2)] * [http://www.bbc.co.uk/radio4/news/inbusiness/inbusiness_20060921.shtml Plug in Cars] In Business, Radio 4, September 2006 * [http://www.greenvehiclenews.com/content/view/400/28/ Green Vehicle News on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.latimes.com/classified/automotive/highway1/la-hy-neil29nov29,0,7930203.story?coll=la-home-headlines Tesla Roadster review] by L.A. Times' Pulitzer-winning auto critic Dan Neil * [http://usfmbapodcast.com/2007/01/28/13-ceo-of-tesla-motors-the-fastest-electric-car-on-earth/ USF MBA Podcast, Jan 28th, 2007] Audio presentation by Tesla's Co-Founder & CEO * [http://www.newsweek.com/id/55405 Green Dream: The Electric Tesla Roadster] An October 2007 update from Newsweek.com [[Category:Companies based in San Mateo County]] [[Category:Companies established in 2003]] [[Category:Motor vehicle manufacturers of the United States]] [[Category:Car manufacturers]] [[Category:Battery electric vehicle manufacturers]] [[Category:Silicon Valley electric vehicles]] [[de:Tesla Motors]] [[eo:Tesla Motors]] [[fr:Tesla Motors]] [[it:Tesla Motors]] [[lt:Tesla Motors]] [[nl:Tesla (automerk)]] [[no:Tesla Motors]] [[pl:Tesla Motors]] [[pt:Tesla Motors]] [[ro:Tesla Motors]] [[fi:Tesla Motors]] [[sv:Tesla Motors]] [[tr:Tesla motors]]

{{Infobox Company | company_name = Tesla Motors, Inc. | company_logo = [[Image:Teslalogo.PNG|center|170px]] | company_type = [[Private company|Private]] | foundation = 2003 | location = [[San Carlos, California]], USA | key_people = [[Ze'ev Drori]], CEO
Michael Marks, Former CEO
[[Martin Eberhard]], co-founder, former CEO (2003-2007 -- no longer with the company)
[[Marc Tarpenning]], co-founder, former Vice President of Engineering (no longer with the company)
[[Elon Musk]], Chairman of the Board
[[Jb straubel|JB Straubel]], CTO | industry = [[Automobile|Automotive]] | products = [[Tesla Roadster]] | revenue = | operating_income = | net_income = | num_employees = 200 + | parent = | subsid = | homepage = http://www.teslamotors.com | slogan = "Burn rubber, not gasoline" }} '''Tesla Motors, Inc.''' is a [[Silicon Valley]] [[automobile]] startup company focusing on the production of high performance, consumer-oriented [[battery electric vehicle|electric vehicle]]s. The firm was started in the summer of 2003 by [[engineer]]s [[Martin Eberhard]] and [[Marc Tarpenning]] in [[San Carlos, California|San Carlos]], and has since grown to include several team members with extensive computer, electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds from around the world. Tesla Motors is named after Serbian inventor [[Nikola Tesla]]. == Financing and Organization== The initial, Series A investment round of USD$7.5 million was led by [[PayPal]] co-founder [[Elon Musk]] in April 2004 and included Compass Technology Partners. Mr. Musk became Tesla's Chairman of the Board and in February 2005, led Tesla Motors' second, USD$13 million Series B investment round which added Valor Equity Partners to the funding team. Mr. Musk co-led the third, USD$40 million Series C round in May 2006 along with [[VantagePoint Venture Partners]]. Tesla's third round included investment from prominent entrepreneurs including [[Google]] co-founders [[Sergey Brin]] & [[Larry Page]], former [[eBay]] President [[Jeff Skoll]], Hyatt heir Nick Pritzker and added the VC firms Draper, Fisher, Jurvetson, Capricorn Management and The Bay Area Equity Fund Managed by JP Morgan.{{cite web |url=http://www.teslamotors.com/display_data/pressguild.swf |title=The Future of Cars is Electric |author=[[Martin Eberhard]] |date=2007-08-07 |quote="Video available from [http://video.google.com/videoplay?docid=-6124789175148491596 Google Video]" }} The fourth, Series D round in May 2007 added another USD$45 million and brought the total investments to over $105 million USD through private financing. Elon Musk, who was President of PayPal before it was bought by eBay, has contributed $37 million of his own money to the company. In August, 2007 [[Martin Eberhard]] was replaced by an interim CEO, Michael Marks. In December 2007, Ze'ev Drori became the permanent CEO and President of Tesla Motors. As of January 2008, Tesla Motors had quietly fired several key personnel that had been involved from the inception after a performance review by the new CEO. {{cite web |url=http://www.teslamotors.com/blog2/?p=52 |title=The Next Leg of the Race |date=2007-08-15 }} {{cite web |url=http://www.edmunds.com/insideline/do/News/articleId=124272 |title="Stealth Bloodbath" Roils Tesla Motors |author=Anita Lienert, Correspondent |publisher=Edmund's Inside Line |date=2008-01-11 |accessdate=2008-02-08 }} == Current models == === Tesla Roadster === [[Image:TeslaRoadster-front.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tesla Roadster]] Tesla Motors' first production vehicle, the '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''', is an all-electric sports car. According to official test results from the Environmental Protection Agency, the car has a range of 221 miles (356 km). The company states that the Tesla Roadster accelerates from 0 to 60 mph (100 km/h) in less than 4 seconds, and a top speed of 125 mph (201 km/h) (limited for safety). The cost of powering the vehicle is estimated at 2 cents per mile.[http://www.teslamotors.com/ Tesla Motors] Prototypes were introduced to the public in July 2006, and the Tesla Roadster was featured on the cover of [[Time Magazine]] in December 2006 as the recipient of the magazine's "Best Inventions 2006 - Transportation Invention" award.[http://www.time.com/time/2006/techguide/bestinventions/ TIME Best Inventions 2006] Demand has been high for the first "Signature One Hundred" set of fully equipped Roadsters, which sold out in less than three weeks,[http://www.megawattmotorworks.com/display.asp?dismode=article&artid=293 Tesla Roadster ‘Signature One Hundred’ Series Sells Out @ Megawatt Motorworks] and the second hundred sold out by October, 2007. As of August 8 2007, more than 550 Tesla Roadsters have been reserved.{{cite news | publisher = Green Car Congress | date = 2007-05-11 | url = http://www.greencarcongress.com/2007/05/tesla_motors_se.html#more | format = [[HTML]] | accessdate = 2007-05-11 | title=Tesla Motors Secures $45 Million Series D Round | language=English }} The first production model was delivered to Elon Musk, Chairman of the Board, in February, 2008 and were in general production by [[March 17]][[2008]].{{cite web |url=http://www.teslamotors.com/blog2/?p=57 |title=We have begun regular production of the Tesla Roadster |publisher=Tesla Motors |date=[[March 17]], [[2008]] |accessdate=2008-03-20 }} Tesla plans to offer home roof mounted solar-[[photovoltaic]] systems through [http://www.solarcity.com/ Solar City] that will offset power used by the home charger, allowing 50 miles (80 km) of travel per day without burdening the [[power grid]], thus making the package "energy positive" for a driver whose average daily mileage is less than that.{{Fact|date=March 2008}} ==Planned models== ===Sports sedan=== Tesla is also currently working on an announced but unrevealed [[sedan]], codenamed ''"[[Tesla WhiteStar|WhiteStar]]"'', which may be introduced in 2009 as a 2010 model. It is being designed as an alternative to cars such as the [[BMW 5 Series]] and the [[Audi A6]], with an estimated price of $50,000-70,000.[http://news.windingroad.com/auto-news/tesla-motors-planning-electrifying-new-sedan/ WindingRoad.com] WhiteStar is to be built in a new plant in [[New Mexico]]. ===Future models=== Future plans include a more affordable third model. The development and production of this future model, codenamed ''"[[Tesla BlueStar|BlueStar]]"'', will be funded by profits from the WhiteStar sedan. According to Tesla, if everything goes according to plan, BlueStar will be released in 2012 and cost around $30,000.[http://www.hybridtactics.com/category/tesla-bluestar BusinessWeek profiles Tesla Motors] == Facilities == ===Headquarters=== Tesla Motors' headquarters are located in Silicon Valley, [[San Carlos, California]]. Much of the development of the Tesla Roadster occurred here. ===United Kingdom=== Tesla Motors has facilities in [[England]] related to the design and assembly of the Tesla Roadster. The Tesla Roadster is currently being assembled by [[Lotus Cars]] in Hethel, England. ===Taiwan=== Tesla Motors has facilities in [[Taiwan]] for motor production. These will be used in the Tesla Roadster and the Tesla WhiteStar. ===Michigan Technical Center=== The Michigan Technical Center, located in [[Rochester Hills, Michigan]], opened January 26, 2007. It is a {{convert|19240|sqft|sqm|sing=on}} facility located at 1840 Enterprise Drive. Approximately $47.7 million will be spent on tooling. The primary focus of the facility is on research & development for future Tesla products. The first priority is the four-door electric sports sedan project codenamed “WhiteStar”. It will be a four door, five-passenger, lightweight, high-performance sedan planned for production around 2009. BlueStar, the more-affordable third model, will also be developed here. The region has an existing base of automotive companies, facilities and engineering talent, with thousands of highly experienced automotive experts. Utilizing these existing resources will reduce costly investments for Tesla Motors. ===Tesla Motors Assembly Facility (Albuquerque)=== Tesla Motors is planning to construct a new plant for mass production of the [[Tesla WhiteStar]] in [[Albuquerque, New Mexico]]. A total capital investment of $35 million is projected to be spent on the {{convert|150000|sqft|sqm|sing=on}} plant, which would provide employment for over 400 people. Competition for the plant was intense, as several states including California, Arizona, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Michigan were vying for the investment. This facility will be the world's first factory dedicated to the mass production of electric vehicles. The company intends to produce at least 10,000 vehicles per year.[http://teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=257 TeslaMotors.com - announcement of Albuquerque plant] [[SunCal]], a land development company, has pledged to provide up to 75 acres (300,000 m²) of land next to the initial site at no cost, if Tesla Motors undertakes a major expansion in the future. Construction was originally planned to begin in April 2007, with completion expected by 2009, including tooling. Tesla Motors has stated that it intends to offer the Tesla WhiteStar for sale in the Fall of 2009. ==Service Centers== Service Centers for the Tesla Roadster are planned for the following United States Metropolitan Areas: *[[Los Angeles, California]] *[[San Francisco Bay Area|San Francisco Bay Area, California]] - 300 El Camino Real, [[Menlo Park, California|Menlo Park]][http://www.paloaltodailynews.com/article/2007-8-2-mp-tesla Palo Alto Daily News] *[[New York, New York]] *[[Miami, Florida]] *[[Chicago, Illinois]] Tesla Motors has stated that it will build additional service centers over the next few years in order to support sales of its next vehicle, the sports sedan currently codenamed the Tesla WhiteStar. "To do 10,000 units for WhiteStar, we need to be in a lot more places," said [[Darryl Siry]], Vice president of Marketing. Planning is currently underway for an additional fifteen service centers in major metropolitan locations.[http://pogue.blogs.nytimes.com/2007/04/05/solving-the-car-propulsion-problem/ Solving the Car-Propulsion Problem - Pogue’s Posts - Technology - New York Times Blog] There are currently no planned service center locations outside of the United States. There is minimal maintenance required of an electric vehicle. There are no oil changes and brake maintenance is minor due to [[regenerative braking]]. Transmission, brake, and cooling system fluid changes will be required as for gasoline-engine cars. ==Partners== Tesla uses several domestic and overseas suppliers and partners. ===AC Propulsion=== Tesla Motors licensed [[AC Propulsion]]'s Reductive Charging(tm) patent, which integrates the charging electronics into the inverter in a way that reduces mass and complexity. Tesla Motors has designed and builds its own power electronics, motor, and other drivetrain components that incorporate this licensed technology from AC Propulsion. ===Lotus Cars=== Tesla has a significant relationship with [[Lotus Cars]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the Tesla Roadster, then codenamed ''Dark Star'', which Lotus's design studio won. The Roadster includes several components from the [[Lotus Elise]] like the windshield and air bag systems, but neither the chassis nor any of the body panels are sourced from Lotus Cars. The chassis design is licensed from Lotus, based on the Elise chassis, was built using the same Lotus software tools used to build the Elise chassis, and is built by the same Norwegian manufacturer. Similarly, the body panels were designed under contract by Barney Hatt of Lotus Design Studios in cooperation with Tesla, but are manufactured from carbon fiber by a third party vendor. Final assembly of the Tesla Roadster is done at Lotus Cars in Hethel, England by both Lotus and Tesla employees. ===Germany=== The brakes and airbags of the Tesla Roadster are made in Germany. ===Norway=== The chassis of the Tesla Roadster is made in Norway. ===Thailand=== The batteries are assembled into sheets for integration into the Energy Storage System by a Tesla supplier in [[Thailand]].[http://www.forbes.com/technology/2007/03/18/tesla-electric-car-tech-cz_ec_0319valleyletter.html?partner=rss Can Silicon Valley Reinvent The Car? - Forbes.com] ==Lawsuits== In March 2008 it was announced that Tesla has filed a lawsuit against [[Fisker Automotive]] saying Henrik Fisker "stole design ideas and confidential information related to the design of hybrid and electric cars". Tesla hired Fisker to design the Whitestar sedan but decided against the design as it was, according to Tesla chairman Elon Musk, "substandard".[http://www.autoblog.com/2008/04/15/tesla-files-suit-against-fisker-automotive/ Autoblog - Tesla files suit against Fisker Automotive] Also in March 2008, [[Magna International]] filed a lawsuit against Tesla claiming that it was never paid for services rendered. Tesla hired Magna to help design a 2-speed transmission for it's Roadster (a transmission they decided not to use).[http://www.autoblog.com/2008/04/16/tables-turned-tesla-motors-sued-by-transmission-supplier-magna/ Autoblog - Tesla Motors Sued By Transmission Supplier Magna] ==Tesla Energy Group== Interest in Tesla Motors' battery technology led to the formation of a division called the '''Tesla Energy Group'''. [[Think Nordic|Think Global]] was to be the first customer, placing an order for development and delivery of battery packs for their electric cars. However, Tesla put their plans on hold due to delays in production and engineering, and Think has accordingly found a new battery supplier, [[EnerDel]]. {{cite press release | publisher = [[Tesla Motors]] | date = 2007-05-22 | url = http://www.teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=509 | format = [[HTML]] | title= Tesla Energy Group, a New Division of Tesla Motors, Signs Development and Supply Agreement Worth $43 Million with Think of Norway |language=English }} == Moniker == Tesla Motors is named after [[Nikola Tesla]], a [[Serbs|Serbian]] [[inventor]], [[electrical engineer]] and [[scientist]]. The principal visionary and inventor of [[alternating current]], Tesla's designs and ideas changed the world, providing a stable means of delivering electricity over enormous distances; the ''[[War of Currents]]'' with [[Thomas Edison|Edison]] over this issue went on for years with Tesla and [[George Westinghouse]] ultimately triumphing. The Tesla Roadster's power plant is basically a Tesla [[three-phase electric power|three-phase]] [[AC motor|AC induction motor]]. ==Board of Directors== * [[Elon Musk]] - Chairman of the board of directors, former President of [[Paypal]], founder and CEO of [[SpaceX]] * [[Ze'ev Drori]] - CEO of Tesla Motors * [[Ira Ehrenpreis]] - General Partner, [[Technology Partners]] * [[Antonio J. Gracias]] - CEO and Chairman of the Investment Committee at [[Valor Equity Partners]] * [[Jim Marver]] - Co-founder and managing partner, [[VantagePoint Venture Partners]] * [[Kimbal Musk]] - CEO of [[Medium, Inc.]] * [[Steve Westly]] - Former Controller for California and founder of [[The Westly Group]] * [[Laurie Yoler]] - Managing Director, [[GrowthPoint Technology Partners]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== * [http://www.teslamotors.com/ Official Tesla Motors website] * [http://edcorner.stanford.edu/authorMaterialInfo.html?mid=1862&author=253 Distribution plan for the new Electric Car], Martin Eberhard speaks at Stanford * [http://www.channel4.com/4car/news/news-story.jsp?news_id=14656 Channel 4 news story] (contains speculative material and rumors) * [http://www.guardian.co.uk/cars/story/0,,1855488,00.html The Guardian newspaper on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_motors_ev.php Tesla Motors: Affordable Electric Cars are Coming] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster.php Tesla Roadster: The Electric Car that Redefines "Power" (Part 1)] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster2.php Tesla Roadster: New Power to the People (Part 2)] * [http://www.bbc.co.uk/radio4/news/inbusiness/inbusiness_20060921.shtml Plug in Cars] In Business, Radio 4, September 2006 * [http://www.greenvehiclenews.com/content/view/400/28/ Green Vehicle News on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.latimes.com/classified/automotive/highway1/la-hy-neil29nov29,0,7930203.story?coll=la-home-headlines Tesla Roadster review] by L.A. Times' Pulitzer-winning auto critic Dan Neil * [http://usfmbapodcast.com/2007/01/28/13-ceo-of-tesla-motors-the-fastest-electric-car-on-earth/ USF MBA Podcast, Jan 28th, 2007] Audio presentation by Tesla's Co-Founder & CEO * [http://www.newsweek.com/id/55405 Green Dream: The Electric Tesla Roadster] An October 2007 update from Newsweek.com [[Category:Companies based in San Mateo County]] [[Category:Companies established in 2003]] [[Category:Motor vehicle manufacturers of the United States]] [[Category:Car manufacturers]] [[Category:Battery electric vehicle manufacturers]] [[Category:Silicon Valley electric vehicles]] [[de:Tesla Motors]] [[eo:Tesla Motors]] [[fr:Tesla Motors]] [[it:Tesla Motors]] [[lt:Tesla Motors]] [[nl:Tesla (automerk)]] [[no:Tesla Motors]] [[pl:Tesla Motors]] [[pt:Tesla Motors]] [[ro:Tesla Motors]] [[fi:Tesla Motors]] [[sv:Tesla Motors]] [[tr:Tesla motors]]

The company intends to produce at least 10,000 vehicles per year.[http://teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=257 TeslaMotors.com - announcement of Albuquerque plant] [[SunCal]], a land development company, has pledged to provide up to 75 acres (300,000 m²) of land next to the initial site at no cost, if Tesla Motors undertakes a major expansion in the future. Construction was originally planned to begin in April 2007, with completion expected by 2009, including tooling. Tesla Motors has stated that it intends to offer the Tesla WhiteStar for sale in the Fall of 2009. ==Service Centers== Service Centers for the Tesla Roadster are planned for the following United States Metropolitan Areas: *[[Los Angeles, California]] *[[San Francisco Bay Area|San Francisco Bay Area, California]] - 300 El Camino Real, [[Menlo Park, California|Menlo Park]][http://www.paloaltodailynews.com/article/2007-8-2-mp-tesla Palo Alto Daily News] *[[New York, New York]] *[[Miami, Florida]] *[[Chicago, Illinois]] Tesla Motors has stated that it will build additional service centers over the next few years in order to support sales of its next vehicle, the sports sedan currently codenamed the Tesla WhiteStar. "To do 10,000 units for WhiteStar, we need to be in a lot more places," said [[Darryl Siry]], Vice President of Marketing. Planning is currently underway for an additional fifteen service centers in major metropolitan locations.[http://pogue.blogs.nytimes.com/2007/04/05/solving-the-car-propulsion-problem/ Solving the Car-Propulsion Problem - Pogue’s Posts - Technology - New York Times Blog] There are currently no planned service center locations outside of the United States. There is minimal maintenance required of an electric vehicle. There are no oil changes and brake maintenance is minor due to [[regenerative braking]]. Transmission, brake, and cooling system fluid changes will be required as for gasoline-engine cars. ==Partners== Tesla uses several domestic and overseas suppliers and partners. ===AC Propulsion=== Tesla Motors licensed [[AC Propulsion]]'s Reductive Charging(tm) patent, which integrates the charging electronics into the inverter in a way that reduces mass and complexity. Tesla Motors has designed and builds its own power electronics, motor, and other drivetrain components that incorporate this licensed technology from AC Propulsion. ===Lotus Cars=== Tesla has a significant relationship with [[Lotus Cars]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the Tesla Roadster, then codenamed ''Dark Star'', which Lotus's design studio won. The Roadster includes several components from the [[Lotus Elise]] like the windshield and air bag systems, but neither the chassis nor any of the body panels are sourced from Lotus Cars. The chassis design is licensed from Lotus, based on the Elise chassis, was built using the same Lotus software tools used to build the Elise chassis, and is built by the same Norwegian manufacturer. Similarly, the body panels were designed under contract by Barney Hatt of Lotus Design Studios in cooperation with Tesla, but are manufactured from carbon fiber by a third party vendor. Final assembly of the Tesla Roadster is done at Lotus Cars in Hethel, England by both Lotus and Tesla employees. ===Germany=== The brakes and airbags of the Tesla Roadster are made in Germany. ===Norway=== The chassis of the Tesla Roadster is made in Norway. ===Thailand=== The batteries are assembled into sheets for integration into the Energy Storage System by a Tesla supplier in [[Thailand]].[http://www.forbes.com/technology/2007/03/18/tesla-electric-car-tech-cz_ec_0319valleyletter.html?partner=rss Can Silicon Valley Reinvent The Car? - Forbes.com] ==Lawsuits== In March 2008 it was announced that Tesla has filed a lawsuit against [[Fisker Automotive]] saying Henrik Fisker "stole design ideas and confidential information related to the design of hybrid and electric cars". Tesla hired Fisker to design the WhiteStar sedan but decided against the design as it was, according to Tesla chairman Elon Musk, "substandard".[http://www.autoblog.com/2008/04/15/tesla-files-suit-against-fisker-automotive/ Autoblog - Tesla files suit against Fisker Automotive] Also in March 2008, [[Magna International]] filed a lawsuit against Tesla claiming that it was never paid for services rendered. Tesla hired Magna to help design a 2-speed transmission for its Roadster (a transmission they decided not to use).[http://www.autoblog.com/2008/04/16/tables-turned-tesla-motors-sued-by-transmission-supplier-magna/ Autoblog - Tesla Motors Sued By Transmission Supplier Magna] ==Tesla Energy Group== Interest in Tesla Motors' battery technology led to the formation of a division called the '''Tesla Energy Group'''. [[Think Nordic|Think Global]] was to be the first customer, placing an order for development and delivery of battery packs for their electric cars. However, Tesla put their plans on hold due to delays in production and engineering, and Think has accordingly found a new battery supplier, [[EnerDel]]. {{cite press release | publisher = [[Tesla Motors]] | date = 2007-05-22 | url = http://www.teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=509 | format = [[HTML]] | title= Tesla Energy Group, a New Division of Tesla Motors, Signs Development and Supply Agreement Worth $43 Million with Think of Norway |language=English }} == Moniker == Tesla Motors is named after [[Nikola Tesla]], an [[inventor]], [[electrical engineer]] and [[scientist]]. The principal visionary and inventor of the [[alternating current]] induction motor and polyphase electrical distribution, Tesla's designs and ideas changed the world, providing a stable means of delivering electricity over enormous distances; the ''[[War of Currents]]'' with [[Thomas Edison|Edison]] over this issue went on for years with Tesla and [[George Westinghouse]] ultimately triumphing. The Tesla Roadster's power plant is basically a Tesla [[three-phase electric power|three-phase]] [[AC motor|AC induction motor]]. ==Board of Directors== * [[Elon Musk]] - Chairman of the board of directors, former President of [[Paypal]], founder and CEO of [[SpaceX]] * [[Ze'ev Drori]] - CEO of Tesla Motors * [[Ira Ehrenpreis]] - General Partner, [[Technology Partners]] * [[Antonio J. Gracias]] - CEO and Chairman of the Investment Committee at [[Valor Equity Partners]] * [[Jim Marver]] - Co-founder and managing partner, [[VantagePoint Venture Partners]] * [[Kimbal Musk]] - CEO of [[Medium, Inc.]] * [[Steve Westly]] - Former Controller for California and founder of [[The Westly Group]] * [[Laurie Yoler]] - Managing Director, [[GrowthPoint Technology Partners]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== * [http://www.teslamotors.com/ Official Tesla Motors website] * [http://edcorner.stanford.edu/authorMaterialInfo.html?mid=1862&author=253 Distribution plan for the new Electric Car], Martin Eberhard speaks at Stanford * [http://www.channel4.com/4car/news/news-story.jsp?news_id=14656 Channel 4 news story] (contains speculative material and rumors) * [http://www.guardian.co.uk/cars/story/0,,1855488,00.html The Guardian newspaper on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_motors_ev.php Tesla Motors: Affordable Electric Cars are Coming] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster.php Tesla Roadster: The Electric Car that Redefines "Power" (Part 1)] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster2.php Tesla Roadster: New Power to the People (Part 2)] * [http://www.bbc.co.uk/radio4/news/inbusiness/inbusiness_20060921.shtml Plug in Cars] In Business, Radio 4, September 2006 * [http://www.greenvehiclenews.com/content/view/400/28/ Green Vehicle News on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.latimes.com/classified/automotive/highway1/la-hy-neil29nov29,0,7930203.story?coll=la-home-headlines Tesla Roadster review] by L.A. Times' Pulitzer-winning auto critic Dan Neil * [http://usfmbapodcast.com/2007/01/28/13-ceo-of-tesla-motors-the-fastest-electric-car-on-earth/ USF MBA Podcast, Jan 28th, 2007] Audio presentation by Tesla's Co-Founder & CEO * [http://www.newsweek.com/id/55405 Green Dream: The Electric Tesla Roadster] An October 2007 update from Newsweek.com [[Category:Companies based in San Mateo County]] [[Category:Companies established in 2003]] [[Category:Motor vehicle manufacturers of the United States]] [[Category:Car manufacturers]] [[Category:Battery electric vehicle manufacturers]] [[Category:Silicon Valley electric vehicles]] [[de:Tesla Motors]] [[eo:Tesla Motors]] [[fr:Tesla Motors]] [[it:Tesla Motors]] [[lt:Tesla Motors]] [[nl:Tesla (automerk)]] [[no:Tesla Motors]] [[pl:Tesla Motors]] [[pt:Tesla Motors]] [[ro:Tesla Motors]] [[fi:Tesla Motors]] [[sv:Tesla Motors]] [[tr:Tesla motors]]

{{Infobox Company | company_name = Tesla Motors, Inc. | company_logo = [[Image:Teslalogo.PNG|center|200px]] | company_type = [[Private company|Private]] | foundation = 2003 | location = [[San Carlos, California]], USA | key_people = [[Ze'ev Drori]], CEO
Michael Marks, Former CEO
[[Martin Eberhard]], co-founder, former CEO (2003-2007 -- no longer with the company)
[[Marc Tarpenning]], co-founder, former Vice President of Engineering (no longer with the company)
[[Elon Musk]], Chairman of the Board
[[JB Straubel]], CTO | industry = [[Automobile|Automotive]] | products = [[Tesla Roadster]] | revenue = | operating_income = | net_income = | num_employees = 200 + | parent = | subsid = | homepage = http://www.teslamotors.com/ | slogan = "Burn rubber, not gasoline" }} '''Tesla Motors, Inc.''' is a [[Silicon Valley]] [[automobile]] startup company focusing on the production of high performance, consumer-oriented [[battery electric vehicle|electric vehicle]]s. The firm was started in the summer of 2003 by [[engineer]]s [[Martin Eberhard]] and [[Marc Tarpenning]] in [[San Carlos, California|San Carlos]], and has since grown to include several team members with extensive computer, electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds from around the world. Tesla Motors is named after inventor [[Nikola Tesla]]. == Financing and Organization== The initial, Series A investment round of USD$7.5 million was led by [[PayPal]] co-founder [[Elon Musk]] in April 2004 and included Compass Technology Partners. Mr. Musk became Tesla's Chairman of the Board and in February 2005, led Tesla Motors' second, USD$13 million Series B investment round which added Valor Equity Partners to the funding team. Mr. Musk co-led the third, USD$40 million Series C round in May 2006 along with [[VantagePoint Venture Partners]]. Tesla's third round included investment from prominent entrepreneurs including [[Google]] co-founders [[Sergey Brin]] & [[Larry Page]], former [[eBay]] President [[Jeff Skoll]], Hyatt heir Nick Pritzker and added the VC firms Draper, Fisher, Jurvetson, Capricorn Management and The Bay Area Equity Fund Managed by JP Morgan.{{cite web |url=http://www.teslamotors.com/display_data/pressguild.swf |title=The Future of Cars is Electric |author=[[Martin Eberhard]] |date=2007-08-07 |quote="Video available from [http://video.google.com/videoplay?docid=-6124789175148491596 Google Video]" }} The fourth, Series D round in May 2007 added another USD$45 million and brought the total investments to over $105 million USD through private financing. Elon Musk, who was President of PayPal before it was bought by eBay, has contributed $37 million of his own money to the company. In August, 2007 [[Martin Eberhard]] was replaced by an interim CEO, Michael Marks. In December 2007, Ze'ev Drori became the permanent CEO and President of Tesla Motors. As of January 2008, Tesla Motors had quietly fired several key personnel that had been involved from the inception after a performance review by the new CEO. {{cite web |url=http://www.teslamotors.com/blog2/?p=52 |title=The Next Leg of the Race |date=2007-08-15 }} {{cite web |url=http://www.edmunds.com/insideline/do/News/articleId=124272 |title="Stealth Bloodbath" Roils Tesla Motors |author=Anita Lienert, Correspondent |publisher=Edmund's Inside Line |date=2008-01-11 |accessdate=2008-02-08 }} == Current models == [[Image:TeslaRoadster-front.jpg|thumb|Tesla Roadster]] Tesla Motors' first production vehicle, the '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''', is an all-electric sports car. According to test results from an [[Environmental Protection Agency|EPA]] certified laboratory, the car has a range of 221 miles (356 km). The company and reviewers state that the Tesla Roadster accelerates from 0 to 60 mph (100 km/h) in less than 4 seconds, and has a top speed of 125 mph (201 km/h) (limited for safety). The cost of powering the vehicle is estimated at US$0.02 per mile.[http://www.teslamotors.com/ Tesla Motors] Prototypes were introduced to the public in July 2006, and the Tesla Roadster was featured on the cover of [[Time Magazine]] in December 2006 as the recipient of the magazine's "Best Inventions 2006 - Transportation Invention" award.[http://www.time.com/time/2006/techguide/bestinventions/ TIME Best Inventions 2006] Demand has been high for the first "Signature One Hundred" set of fully equipped Roadsters, which sold out in less than three weeks,[http://www.megawattmotorworks.com/display.asp?dismode=article&artid=293 Tesla Roadster ‘Signature One Hundred’ Series Sells Out @ Megawatt Motorworks] and the second hundred sold out by October, 2007. As of May 2 2008, more than 600 Tesla Roadsters have been reserved and 400 more are on the waiting list.{{cite news | publisher = AutoWeek | date = 2008-05-02 | url = http://www.autoweek.com/apps/pbcs.dll/article?AID=/20080502/FREE/422389269/1023/rss01&rssfeed=rss01 | format = [[HTML]] | accessdate = 2008-05-05 | title=Tesla opens first dealership in Los Angeles | language=English }} The first production model was delivered to Elon Musk, Chairman of the Board, in February, 2008 and were in general production by [[March 17]][[2008]].{{cite web |url=http://www.teslamotors.com/blog2/?p=57 |title=We have begun regular production of the Tesla Roadster |publisher=Tesla Motors |date=[[March 17]], [[2008]] |accessdate=2008-03-20 }} Tesla plans to offer home roof mounted solar-[[photovoltaic]] systems through [http://www.solarcity.com/ Solar City], another Elon Musk–funded company, that will offset power used by the home charger, allowing 50 miles (80 km) of travel per day without burdening the [[power grid]], thus making the package "energy positive" for a driver whose average daily mileage is less than that.{{Fact|date=March 2008}} ==Planned models== Tesla is also currently working on an announced but unrevealed [[sedan]], codenamed ''"[[Tesla WhiteStar|WhiteStar]]"'', which may be introduced in 2009 as a 2010 model. It is being designed as an alternative to cars such as the [[BMW 5 Series]] and the [[Audi A6]], with an estimated price of $50,000-70,000.[http://news.windingroad.com/auto-news/tesla-motors-planning-electrifying-new-sedan/ WindingRoad.com] WhiteStar is to be built in a new plant in [[New Mexico]]. Future plans include a more affordable third model. The development and production of this future model, codenamed ''"[[Tesla BlueStar|BlueStar]]"'', will be funded by profits from the WhiteStar sedan. According to Tesla, if everything goes according to plan, BlueStar will be released in 2012 and cost around $30,000.[http://www.hybridtactics.com/category/tesla-bluestar BusinessWeek profiles Tesla Motors] == Facilities == Tesla Motors' headquarters are located in Silicon Valley, [[San Carlos, California]]. Much of the development of the Tesla Roadster occurred here. === Europe === Tesla Motors has facilities in [[England]] related to the design and assembly of the Tesla Roadster. The Tesla Roadster is currently being assembled by [[Lotus Cars]] in Hethel, England. === Asia === Tesla Motors has facilities in [[Taiwan]] for motor production. These will be used in the Tesla Roadster and the Tesla WhiteStar. === United States === The Michigan Technical Center, located in [[Rochester Hills, Michigan]], opened January 26, 2007. It is a {{convert|19240|sqft|sqm|sing=on}} facility located at 1840 Enterprise Drive. Approximately $47.7 million will be spent on tooling. The primary focus of the facility is on research & development for future Tesla products. The first priority is the four-door electric sports sedan project codenamed “WhiteStar”. It will be a four door, five-passenger, lightweight, high-performance sedan planned for production around 2009. BlueStar, the more-affordable third model, will also be developed here. The region has an existing base of automotive companies, facilities and engineering talent, with thousands of highly experienced automotive experts. Utilizing these existing resources will reduce costly investments for Tesla Motors. Tesla Motors is planning to construct a new plant for mass production of the [[Tesla WhiteStar]] in [[Albuquerque, New Mexico]]. A total capital investment of $35 million is projected to be spent on the {{convert|150000|sqft|sqm|sing=on}} plant, which would provide employment for over 400 people. Competition for the plant was intense, as several states including California, Arizona, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Michigan were vying for the investment. This facility will be the world's first factory dedicated to the mass production of electric vehicles. As of May 2 2008, more than 600 Tesla Roadsters have been reserved and 400 more are on the waiting list.{{cite news | publisher = AutoWeek | date = 2008-05-02 | url = http://www.autoweek.com/apps/pbcs.dll/article?AID=/20080502/FREE/422389269/1023/rss01&rssfeed=rss01 | format = [[HTML]] | accessdate = 2008-05-05 | title=Tesla opens first dealership in Los Angeles | language=English }} The first production model was delivered to Elon Musk, Chairman of the Board, in February, 2008 and were in general production by [[March 17]][[2008]].{{cite web |url=http://www.teslamotors.com/blog2/?p=57 |title=We have begun regular production of the Tesla Roadster |publisher=Tesla Motors |date=[[March 17]], [[2008]] |accessdate=2008-03-20 }} Tesla plans to offer home roof mounted solar-[[photovoltaic]] systems through [http://www.solarcity.com/ Solar City], another Elon Musk–funded company, that will offset power used by the home charger, allowing 50 miles (80 km) of travel per day without burdening the [[power grid]], thus making the package "energy positive" for a driver whose average daily mileage is less than that.{{Fact|date=March 2008}} ==Planned models== Tesla is also currently working on an announced but unrevealed [[sedan]], codenamed ''"[[Tesla WhiteStar|WhiteStar]]"'', which may be introduced in 2009 as a 2010 model. It is being designed as an alternative to cars such as the [[BMW 5 Series]] and the [[Audi A6]], with an estimated price of $50,000-70,000.[http://news.windingroad.com/auto-news/tesla-motors-planning-electrifying-new-sedan/ WindingRoad.com] WhiteStar is to be built in a new plant in [[New Mexico]]. Future plans include a more affordable third model. The development and production of this future model, codenamed ''"[[Tesla BlueStar|BlueStar]]"'', will be funded by profits from the WhiteStar sedan. According to Tesla, if everything goes according to plan, BlueStar will be released in 2012 and cost around $30,000.[http://www.hybridtactics.com/category/tesla-bluestar BusinessWeek profiles Tesla Motors] == Facilities == Tesla Motors' headquarters are located in Silicon Valley, [[San Carlos, California]]. Much of the development of the Tesla Roadster occurred here. === Europe === Tesla Motors has facilities in [[England]] related to the design and assembly of the Tesla Roadster. The Tesla Roadster is currently being assembled by [[Lotus Cars]] in Hethel, England. === Asia === Tesla Motors has facilities in [[Taiwan]] for motor production. These will be used in the Tesla Roadster and the Tesla WhiteStar. === United States === The Michigan Technical Center, located in [[Rochester Hills, Michigan]], opened January 26, 2007. It is a {{convert|19240|sqft|sqm|sing=on}} facility located at 1840 Enterprise Drive. Approximately $47.7 million will be spent on tooling. The primary focus of the facility is on research & development for future Tesla products. The first priority is the four-door electric sports sedan project codenamed “WhiteStar”. It will be a four door, five-passenger, lightweight, high-performance sedan planned for production around 2009. BlueStar, the more-affordable third model, will also be developed here. The region has an existing base of automotive companies, facilities and engineering talent, with thousands of highly experienced automotive experts. Utilizing these existing resources will reduce costly investments for Tesla Motors. Tesla Motors is planning to construct a new plant for mass production of the [[Tesla WhiteStar]] in [[Albuquerque, New Mexico]]. A total capital investment of $35 million is projected to be spent on the {{convert|150000|sqft|sqm|sing=on}} plant, which would provide employment for over 400 people. Competition for the plant was intense, as several states including California, Arizona, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Michigan were vying for the investment. This facility will be the world's first factory dedicated to the mass production of electric vehicles. The company intends to produce at least 10,000 vehicles per year.[http://teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=257 TeslaMotors.com - announcement of Albuquerque plant] [[SunCal]], a land development company, has pledged to provide up to 75 acres (300,000 m²) of land next to the initial site at no cost, if Tesla Motors undertakes a major expansion in the future. Construction was originally planned to begin in April 2007, with completion expected by 2009, including tooling. Tesla Motors has stated that it intends to offer the Tesla WhiteStar for sale in the Fall of 2009. ==Service Centers== [[Image:TeslaLA.jpg|thumb|Tesla Motors dealership on Santa Monica Boulevard in [[Los Angeles]], California]] Service Centers for the Tesla Roadster are planned for the following United States Metropolitan Areas: *[[Los Angeles, California]] - 11163 Santa Monica Blvd [http://la.racked.com/archives/2008/05/05/now_open_its_electric_tesla_motors_in_santa_monica.php] *[[San Francisco Bay Area|San Francisco Bay Area, California]] - 300 El Camino Real, [[Menlo Park, California|Menlo Park]][http://www.paloaltodailynews.com/article/2007-8-2-mp-tesla Palo Alto Daily News] *[[New York, New York]] *[[Miami, Florida]] *[[Chicago, Illinois]] *[[Seattle, Washington]] Tesla Motors has stated that it will build additional service centers over the next few years in order to support sales of its next vehicle, the sports sedan currently codenamed the Tesla WhiteStar. "To do 10,000 units for WhiteStar, we need to be in a lot more places," said [[Darryl Siry]], Vice President of Marketing. Planning is currently underway for an additional fifteen service centers in major metropolitan locations.[http://pogue.blogs.nytimes.com/2007/04/05/solving-the-car-propulsion-problem/ Solving the Car-Propulsion Problem - Pogue’s Posts - Technology - New York Times Blog] There are currently no planned service center locations outside of the United States. There is minimal maintenance required of an electric vehicle. There are no oil changes and brake maintenance is minor due to [[regenerative braking]]. Transmission, brake, and cooling system fluid changes will be required as for gasoline-engine cars. ==Partners== Tesla uses several domestic and overseas suppliers and partners. The brakes and airbags of the Tesla Roadster are made in Germany, while the chassis of is made in Norway. The batteries are assembled into sheets for integration into the Energy Storage System by a Tesla supplier in [[Thailand]].[http://www.forbes.com/technology/2007/03/18/tesla-electric-car-tech-cz_ec_0319valleyletter.html?partner=rss Can Silicon Valley Reinvent The Car? - Forbes.com] Tesla Motors licensed [[AC Propulsion]]'s Reductive Charging(tm) patent, which integrates the charging electronics into the inverter in a way that reduces mass and complexity. Tesla Motors has designed and builds its own power electronics, motor, and other drivetrain components that incorporate this licensed technology from AC Propulsion. Tesla has a significant relationship with [[Lotus Cars]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the Tesla Roadster, then codenamed ''Dark Star'', which Lotus's design studio won. The Roadster includes several components from the [[Lotus Elise]] like the windshield and air bag systems, but neither the chassis nor any of the body panels are sourced from Lotus Cars. The chassis design is licensed from Lotus, based on the Elise chassis, was built using the same Lotus software tools used to build the Elise chassis, and is built by the same Norwegian manufacturer. Similarly, the body panels were designed under contract by Barney Hatt of Lotus Design Studios in cooperation with Tesla, but are manufactured from carbon fiber by a third party vendor. Final assembly of the Tesla Roadster is done at Lotus Cars in Hethel, England by both Lotus and Tesla employees. ==Lawsuits== In March 2008 it was announced that Tesla has filed a lawsuit against [[Fisker Automotive]] saying Henrik Fisker "stole design ideas and confidential information related to the design of hybrid and electric cars". Tesla hired Fisker to design the WhiteStar sedan but decided against the design as it was, according to Tesla chairman Elon Musk, "substandard".[http://www.autoblog.com/2008/04/15/tesla-files-suit-against-fisker-automotive/ Autoblog - Tesla files suit against Fisker Automotive] Also in March 2008, [[Magna International]] filed a lawsuit against Tesla claiming that it was never paid for services rendered. Tesla hired Magna to help design a 2-speed transmission for its Roadster (a transmission they decided not to use).[http://www.autoblog.com/2008/04/16/tables-turned-tesla-motors-sued-by-transmission-supplier-magna/ Autoblog - Tesla Motors Sued By Transmission Supplier Magna] ==Tesla Energy Group== Interest in Tesla Motors' battery technology led to the formation of a division called the '''Tesla Energy Group'''. [[Think Nordic|Think Global]] was to be the first customer, placing an order for development and delivery of battery packs for their electric cars. However, Tesla put their plans on hold due to delays in production and engineering, and Think has accordingly found a new battery supplier, [[EnerDel]]. {{cite press release | publisher = [[Tesla Motors]] | date = 2007-05-22 | url = http://www.teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=509 | format = [[HTML]] | title= Tesla Energy Group, a New Division of Tesla Motors, Signs Development and Supply Agreement Worth $43 Million with Think of Norway |language=English }} == Moniker == Tesla Motors is named after [[Nikola Tesla]], an [[inventor]], [[electrical engineer]] and [[scientist]]. The principal visionary and inventor of the [[alternating current]] [[induction motor]] and [[polyphase system]] of electrical distribution, Tesla's designs and ideas changed the world, providing a stable means of delivering electricity over enormous distances; the ''[[War of Currents]]'' with [[Thomas Edison|Edison]] over this issue went on for years with Tesla and [[George Westinghouse]] ultimately triumphing. The Tesla Roadster's power plant is basically a Tesla [[three-phase electric power|three-phase]] [[AC motor|AC induction motor]]. ==Board of Directors== * [[Elon Musk]] - Chairman of the board of directors, former President of [[Paypal]], founder and CEO of [[SpaceX]] * [[Ze'ev Drori]] - CEO of Tesla Motors * [[Ira Ehrenpreis]] - General Partner, [[Technology Partners]] * [[Antonio J. Gracias]] - CEO and Chairman of the Investment Committee at [[Valor Equity Partners]] * [[Jim Marver]] - Co-founder and managing partner, [[VantagePoint Venture Partners]] * [[Kimbal Musk]] - CEO of [[Medium, Inc.]] * [[Steve Westly]] - Former Controller for California and founder of [[The Westly Group]] * [[Laurie Yoler]] - Managing Director, [[GrowthPoint Technology Partners]] == References == {{refs|2}} ==External links== * [http://www.teslamotors.com/ Official Tesla Motors website] * [http://edcorner.stanford.edu/authorMaterialInfo.html?mid=1862&author=253 Distribution plan for the new Electric Car], Martin Eberhard speaks at Stanford * [http://www.channel4.com/4car/news/news-story.jsp?news_id=14656 Channel 4 news story] (contains speculative material and rumors) * [http://www.guardian.co.uk/cars/story/0,,1855488,00.html The Guardian newspaper on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_motors_ev.php Tesla Motors: Affordable Electric Cars are Coming] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster.php Tesla Roadster: The Electric Car that Redefines "Power" (Part 1)] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster2.php Tesla Roadster: New Power to the People (Part 2)] * [http://www.bbc.co.uk/radio4/news/inbusiness/inbusiness_20060921.shtml Plug in Cars] In Business, Radio 4, September 2006 * [http://www.greenvehiclenews.com/content/view/400/28/ Green Vehicle News on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.latimes.com/classified/automotive/highway1/la-hy-neil29nov29,0,7930203.story?coll=la-home-headlines Tesla Roadster review] by L.A. Times' Pulitzer-winning auto critic Dan Neil * [http://usfmbapodcast.com/2007/01/28/13-ceo-of-tesla-motors-the-fastest-electric-car-on-earth/ USF MBA Podcast, Jan 28th, 2007] Audio presentation by Tesla's Co-Founder & CEO * [http://www.newsweek.com/id/55405 Green Dream: The Electric Tesla Roadster] An October 2007 update from Newsweek.com [[Category:Companies based in San Mateo County]] [[Category:Companies established in 2003]] [[Category:Motor vehicle manufacturers of the United States]] [[Category:Car manufacturers]] [[Category:Battery electric vehicle manufacturers]] [[Category:Silicon Valley electric vehicles]] [[Category:Plug-in hybrid vehicle manufacturers]] [[de:Tesla Motors]] [[eo:Tesla Motors]] [[fr:Tesla Motors]] [[hr:Tesla Motors]] [[it:Tesla Motors]] [[lt:Tesla Motors]] [[nl:Tesla (automerk)]] [[ja:テスラモータース]] [[no:Tesla Motors]] [[pl:Tesla Motors]] [[pt:Tesla Motors]] [[ro:Tesla Motors]] [[sk:Tesla Motors]] [[fi:Tesla Motors]] [[sv:Tesla Motors]] [[tr:Tesla Motors]]

{{Infobox Company | company_name = Tesla Motors, Inc. | company_logo = [[Image:Teslalogo.PNG|200px]] | company_type = [[Private company|Private]] | foundation = 2003 | location = [[San Carlos, California]], USA | key_people = [[Ze'ev Drori]] ([[chief executive officer|CEO]])
Michael Marks (former CEO)
[[Martin Eberhard]] (co-founder)
[[Marc Tarpenning]] (co-founder)
[[Elon Musk]] ([[Chairman]])
[[JB Straubel]] ([[chief technical officer|CTO]]) | industry = [[Automobile|Automotive]] | products = [[Tesla Roadster]] | num_employees = 250 + | homepage = http://www.teslamotors.com/ | slogan = Burn rubber, not gasoline }} '''Tesla Motors, Inc.''' is a [[Silicon Valley]] [[automobile]] startup company focusing on the production of high performance, consumer-oriented [[battery electric vehicle|electric vehicle]]s. The firm was started in July, 2003 by [[engineer]]s [[Martin Eberhard]] and [[Marc Tarpenning]] in [[San Carlos, California]] and has since grown to include several team members with extensive computer, electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds from around the world. Tesla Motors is named after inventor [[Nikola Tesla]].{{cite web |url=http://www.teslamotors.com/learn_more/why_tesla.php |title=Why the Name "Tesla"? |publisher=Tesla Motors |accessdate=2008-06-10 }} == Financing and Organization== The initial, Series A investment round of USD$7.5 million was led by [[PayPal]] co-founder [[Elon Musk]] in April 2004 and included Compass Technology Partners. Mr. Musk became Tesla's Chairman of the Board and in February 2005, led Tesla Motors' second, USD$13 million Series B investment round which added Valor Equity Partners to the funding team. Mr. Musk co-led the third, USD$40 million Series C round in May 2006 along with [[VantagePoint Venture Partners]]. Tesla's third round included investment from prominent entrepreneurs including [[Google]] co-founders [[Sergey Brin]] & [[Larry Page]], former [[eBay]] President [[Jeff Skoll]], Hyatt heir Nick Pritzker and added the VC firms Draper, Fisher, Jurvetson, Capricorn Management and The Bay Area Equity Fund Managed by JP Morgan.{{cite web |url=http://www.teslamotors.com/display_data/pressguild.swf |title=The Future of Cars is Electric |author=[[Martin Eberhard]] |date=2007-08-07 |quote="Video available from [http://video.google.com/videoplay?docid=-6124789175148491596 Google Video]" |accessdate=2008-06-10 }} The fourth, Series D round in May 2007 added another USD$45 million and brought the total investments to over $105 million USD through private financing. Elon Musk, who was President of PayPal before it was bought by eBay, has contributed $37 million of his own money to the company. In August, 2007 [[Martin Eberhard]] was replaced by an interim CEO, Michael Marks. In December 2007, Ze'ev Drori became the permanent CEO and President of Tesla Motors. As of January 2008, Tesla Motors had quietly fired several key personnel that had been involved from the inception after a performance review by the new CEO. {{cite web |url=http://www.teslamotors.com/blog2/?p=52 |title=The Next Leg of the Race |date=2007-08-15 }} {{cite web |url=http://www.edmunds.com/insideline/do/News/articleId=124272 |title="Stealth Bloodbath" Roils Tesla Motors |author=Anita Lienert, Correspondent |publisher=Edmund's Inside Line |date=2008-01-11 |accessdate=2008-02-08 }} == Current models == [[Image:TeslaRoadster-front.jpg|thumb|Tesla Roadster]] Tesla Motors' first production vehicle, the '''[[Tesla Roadster]]''', is an all-electric sports car. According to test results from an [[Environmental Protection Agency|EPA]] certified laboratory, the car has a range of 221 miles (356 km) from a battery pack consisting of 6,581 1.7V Lithium-Ion batteries, when ooperated at peak battery longevity, i.e., low to moderate load, using low speeds; the battery pack needs replacing every 100,000 miles, by which time its range will potentially have decreased by 30%. The company and reviewers state that the Tesla Roadster accelerates from 0 to 60 mph (100 km/h) in less than 4 seconds, and has a top speed of 125 mph (201 km/h) (electronically limited for safety). As with all electric motors, the motor produces substantial torque at just above 0 RPM, decreasing with increasing speed; it is at peak power from 0 to 8,000 RPM (this statement is not possible, since it implies that torque decreases exactly inversely with RPM (power is the product of torque and rotation speed) and practical motors do not exhibit such characteristics) and red-line is at 13,000. With such a high redline and broad torque and power curves, it needs only a single gear; the prototypes had a single gear; the production car has a specially-designed transmission with two forward gears, manufacture of which had been an issue for Tesla. The cost of powering the vehicle is estimated at US$0.02 per mile.[http://www.teslamotors.com/ Tesla Motors] Prototypes were introduced to the public in July 2006, and the Tesla Roadster was featured on the cover of [[Time Magazine]] in December 2006 as the recipient of the magazine's "Best Inventions 2006 - Transportation Invention" award.[http://www.time.com/time/2006/techguide/bestinventions/ TIME Best Inventions 2006] Demand has been high for the first "Signature One Hundred" set of fully equipped Roadsters, which sold out in less than three weeks,[http://www.megawattmotorworks.com/display.asp?dismode=article&artid=293 Tesla Roadster ‘Signature One Hundred’ Series Sells Out @ Megawatt Motorworks] and the second hundred sold out by October, 2007. As of May 2 2008, more than 600 Tesla Roadsters have been reserved and 400 more are on the waiting list.{{cite news | publisher = AutoWeek | date = 2008-05-02 | url = http://www.autoweek.com/apps/pbcs.dll/article?AID=/20080502/FREE/422389269/1023/rss01&rssfeed=rss01 | format = [[HTML]] | accessdate = 2008-05-05 | title=Tesla opens first dealership in Los Angeles | language=English }} The first production model was delivered to Elon Musk, Chairman of the Board, in February, 2008 and were in general production by [[March 17]][[2008]].{{cite web |url=http://www.teslamotors.com/blog2/?p=57 |title=We have begun regular production of the Tesla Roadster |publisher=Tesla Motors |date=[[March 17]], [[2008]] |accessdate=2008-03-20 }} Tesla plans to offer home roof mounted solar-[[photovoltaic]] systems through [http://www.solarcity.com/ Solar City], another Elon Musk–funded company, that will offset power used by the home charger, allowing 50 miles (80 km) of travel per day without burdening the [[power grid]], thus making the package "energy positive" for a driver whose average daily mileage is less than that.{{Fact|date=March 2008}} ==Planned models== Tesla is also currently working on an announced but unrevealed [[sedan]], codenamed ''"[[Tesla WhiteStar|WhiteStar]]"'', which may be introduced in 2009 as a 2010 model. {{ infobox company | company_name = Tesla Motors, Inc. | company_logo = [[Image:Teslalogo.PNG|200px]] | company_type = [[Private company|Private]] | foundation = 2003 | location = [[San Carlos, California]], USA | key_people = [[Ze'ev Drori]] ([[chief executive officer|CEO]])
Michael Marks (former CEO)
[[Martin Eberhard]] (co-founder)
[[Marc Tarpenning]] (co-founder)
[[Elon Musk]] ([[Chairman]])
[[JB Straubel]] ([[chief technical officer|CTO]]) | industry = [[Automobile|Automotive]] | products = [[Tesla Roadster]] | num_employees = 250 + | homepage = http://www.teslamotors.com/ | slogan = Burn rubber, not gasoline }} '''Tesla Motors, Inc.''' is a [[Silicon Valley]] [[automobile]] startup company focusing on the production of high performance, consumer-oriented [[battery electric vehicle]]s.{{cite web | url = http://video.google.com/videoplay?docid=-6124789175148491596 | format = [[Flash video]] | title = Martin Eberhard of Tesla Motors speaks to the Motor Press Guild | author = [[Martin Eberhard]] | date = 2007-08-07 | accessdate = 2008-06-22 }} The firm was started in July, 2003 by [[engineer]]s [[Martin Eberhard]] and [[Marc Tarpenning]] in [[San Carlos, California]] and has since grown to include several team members with extensive computer, electrical, and automotive engineering backgrounds from around the world. Tesla Motors is named after [[Serbian American]] [[electrical engineer]] and [[physicist]] [[Nikola Tesla]].{{ cite web | url = http://www.teslamotors.com/learn_more/why_tesla.php | title = Why the Name "Tesla"? | publisher = Tesla Motors | accessdate = 2008-06-10 }} == Financing and organization == The initial, Series A investment round of USD$7.5 million was led by [[PayPal]] co-founder [[Elon Musk]] in April 2004 and included Compass Technology Partners. Mr. Musk became Tesla's Chairman of the Board and in February 2005, led Tesla Motors' second, USD$13 million Series B investment round which added Valor Equity Partners to the funding team. Mr. Musk co-led the third, USD$40 million Series C round in May 2006 along with [[VantagePoint Venture Partners]]. Tesla's third round included investment from prominent entrepreneurs including [[Google]] co-founders [[Sergey Brin]] & [[Larry Page]], former [[eBay]] President [[Jeff Skoll]], Hyatt heir Nick Pritzker and added the VC firms Draper, Fisher, Jurvetson, Capricorn Management and The Bay Area Equity Fund Managed by JP Morgan. The fourth, Series D round in May 2007 added another USD$45 million and brought the total investments to over $105 million USD through private financing. Elon Musk, who was President of PayPal before it was bought by eBay, has contributed $37 million of his own money to the company. In August, 2007 [[Martin Eberhard]] was replaced by an interim CEO, Michael Marks. In December 2007, Ze'ev Drori became the permanent CEO and President of Tesla Motors.{{ cite web | url = http://www.edmunds.com/insideline/do/News/articleId=124272 | title = "Stealth Bloodbath" Roils Tesla Motors | author = Anita Lienert, Correspondent | publisher = Edmund's Inside Line | date = 2008-01-11 | accessdate = 2008-02-08 }} In January 2008, Tesla Motors fired several key personnel that had been involved from the inception after a performance review by the new CEO. {{ cite web | url = http://www.teslamotors.com/blog2/?p=52 | title = The Next Leg of the Race | date = 2007-08-15 }} == Current models == [[Image:TeslaRoadster-front.jpg|thumb|Tesla Roadster]] Tesla Motors' first production vehicle, the [[Tesla Roadster]], is an all-electric sports car. According to test results from an [[Environmental Protection Agency|EPA]] certified laboratory, the car has a range of 221 miles (356 km). The company and reviewers state that the Tesla Roadster accelerates from 0 to 60 mph (100 km/h) in less than 4 seconds, and has a top speed of 125 mph (201 km/h) (limited for safety). The cost of powering the vehicle is estimated at US$0.02 per mile.{{ cite web | url = http://www.time.com/time/2006/techguide/bestinventions/ | title = TIME Best Inventions 2006}} Prototypes were introduced to the public in July 2006, and the Tesla Roadster was featured on the cover of [[Time Magazine]] in December 2006 as the recipient of the magazine's "Best Inventions 2006 - Transportation Invention" award. Demand has been high for the first "Signature One Hundred" set of fully equipped Roadsters, which sold out in less than three weeks,{{ cite web | url = http://www.megawattmotorworks.com/display.asp?dismode=article&artid=293 | title = Tesla Roadster ‘Signature One Hundred’ Series Sells Out | publisher = Megawatt Motorworks }} and the second hundred sold out by October, 2007. As of May 2 2008, more than 600 Tesla Roadsters have been reserved and 400 more are on the waiting list.{{cite news | publisher = AutoWeek | date = 2008-05-02 | url = http://www.autoweek.com/apps/pbcs.dll/article?AID=/20080502/FREE/422389269/1023/rss01&rssfeed=rss01 | accessdate = 2008-05-05 | title=Tesla opens first dealership in Los Angeles }} The first production model was delivered to Elon Musk, Chairman of the Board, in February, 2008 and were in general production by [[March 17]][[2008]].{{ cite web | url = http://www.teslamotors.com/blog2/?p=57 | title = We have begun regular production of the Tesla Roadster | publisher = Tesla Motors | date = [[17 March]] [[2008]] | accessdate = 2008-03-20 }} Tesla plans to offer home roof mounted solar-[[photovoltaic]] systems through [[Solar City]], another Elon Musk-funded company, that will offset power used by the home charger, allowing 50 miles (80 km) of travel per day without burdening the [[power grid]], thus making the package "energy positive" for a driver whose average daily mileage is less than that.{{Fact|date=March 2008}} == Planned models == Tesla is also currently working on an announced but unrevealed [[sedan]], codenamed ''"[[Tesla WhiteStar|WhiteStar]]"'', which may be introduced in 2009 as a 2010 model. It is being designed as an alternative to cars such as the [[BMW 5 Series]] and the [[Audi A6]], with an estimated price of $50,000-70,000.[http://news.windingroad.com/auto-news/tesla-motors-planning-electrifying-new-sedan/ WindingRoad.com] WhiteStar is to be built in a new plant in [[New Mexico]]. Future plans include a more affordable third model. The development and production of this future model, codenamed ''"[[Tesla BlueStar|BlueStar]]"'', will be funded by profits from the WhiteStar sedan. According to Tesla, if everything goes according to plan, BlueStar will be released in 2012 and cost around $30,000.[http://www.hybridtactics.com/category/tesla-bluestar BusinessWeek profiles Tesla Motors] == Facilities == Tesla Motors' headquarters are located in [[San Carlos, California]], where much of the development of the Tesla Roadster occurred. === Europe === Tesla Motors has facilities in [[England]] related to the design and assembly of the Tesla Roadster. The Tesla Roadster is currently being assembled by [[Lotus Cars]] in Hethel, England. === Asia === Tesla Motors has facilities in [[Taiwan]] for motor production. These will be used in the Tesla Roadster and the Tesla WhiteStar. === United States === The Michigan Technical Center, located in [[Rochester Hills, Michigan]], opened January 26, 2007. It is a {{convert|19240|sqft|m2|sing=on}} facility located at 1840 Enterprise Drive. Approximately $47.7 million will be spent on tooling. The primary focus of the facility is on research & development for future Tesla products. The first priority is the four-door electric sports sedan project codenamed “WhiteStar”. It will be a four door, five-passenger, lightweight, high-performance sedan planned for production around 2009. BlueStar, the more-affordable third model, will also be developed here. The region has an existing base of automotive companies, facilities and engineering talent, with thousands of highly experienced automotive experts. Utilizing these existing resources will reduce costly investments for Tesla Motors. Tesla Motors is planning to construct a new plant for mass production of the [[Tesla WhiteStar]] in [[Albuquerque, New Mexico]]. A total capital investment of $35 million is projected to be spent on the {{convert|150000|sqft|m2|sing=on}} plant, which would provide employment for over 400 people. Competition for the plant was intense, as several states including California, Arizona, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Michigan were vying for the investment. This facility will be the world's first factory dedicated to the mass production of electric vehicles. The company intends to produce at least 10,000 vehicles per year.[http://teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=257 TeslaMotors.com - announcement of Albuquerque plant] [[SunCal]], a land development company, has pledged to provide up to 75 acres (300,000 m²) of land next to the initial site at no cost, if Tesla Motors undertakes a major expansion in the future. Construction was originally planned to begin in April 2007, with completion expected by 2009, including tooling. Tesla Motors has stated that it intends to offer the Tesla WhiteStar for sale in the Fall of 2009. == Service Centers == [[Image:TeslaLA.jpg|thumb|Tesla Motors dealership on Santa Monica Boulevard in [[Los Angeles]], California]] Service Centers for the Tesla Roadster are planned for the following United States Metropolitan Areas: * [[Los Angeles, California]] - 11163 Santa Monica Blvd{{ cite web | title = Now Open: It's Electric! Tesla Motors in Santa Monica | url = http://la.racked.com/archives/2008/05/05/now_open_its_electric_tesla_motors_in_santa_monica.php | date = [[5 May]] [[2008]] | author = Tasha Nita Adams | authorlink = http://www.blackburnandsweetzer.blogspot.com/ | publisher = Racked LA | accessdate = 2008-06-22 }} * [[San Francisco Bay Area|San Francisco Bay Area, California]] - 300 El Camino Real, [[Menlo Park, California]]{{ cite web | url = http://www.paloaltodailynews.com/article/2007-8-2-mp-tesla | title = Elecrifying move: Tesla Motors plans to set up hub in Menlo Park | accessdate = 2008-06-22 | first = Banks | last = Albach | date = [[2 August]] [[2007]] | publisher = Palo Alto Daily News }} * [[New York, New York]] * [[Miami, Florida]] * [[Chicago, Illinois]] * [[Seattle, Washington]] Tesla Motors has stated that it will build additional service centers over the next few years in order to support sales of its next vehicle, the sports sedan currently codenamed the Tesla WhiteStar. "To do 10,000 units for WhiteStar, we need to be in a lot more places," said [[Darryl Siry]], Vice President of Marketing. Planning is currently underway for an additional fifteen service centers in major metropolitan locations.{{ cite web | url = http://pogue.blogs.nytimes.com/2007/04/05/solving-the-car-propulsion-problem/ | title = Solving the Car-Propulsion Problem | work = Pogue’s Posts - Technology | first = David | last = Pogue | publisher = New York Times }} There are currently no planned service center locations outside of the United States. There is minimal maintenance required of an electric vehicle. There are no oil changes and brake maintenance is minor due to [[regenerative braking]]. Transmission, brake, and cooling system fluid changes will be required as for gasoline-engine cars. == Partners == Tesla uses several domestic and overseas suppliers and partners. The brakes and airbags of the Tesla Roadster are made in Germany, while the chassis is made in Norway. The batteries are assembled into sheets for integration into the Energy Storage System by a Tesla supplier in [[Thailand]].[http://www.forbes.com/technology/2007/03/18/tesla-electric-car-tech-cz_ec_0319valleyletter.html?partner=rss Can Silicon Valley Reinvent The Car? - Forbes.com] Tesla Motors licensed [[AC Propulsion]]'s Reductive Charging(tm) patent, which integrates the charging electronics into the inverter in a way that reduces mass and complexity. Tesla Motors has designed and builds its own power electronics, motor, and other drivetrain components that incorporate this licensed technology from AC Propulsion. Tesla has a significant relationship with [[Lotus Cars]]. A design contest was held for the final styling of the Tesla Roadster, then codenamed ''Dark Star'', which Lotus's design studio won. The Roadster includes several components from the [[Lotus Elise]] like the windshield and air bag systems, but neither the chassis nor any of the body panels are sourced from Lotus Cars. The chassis design is licensed from Lotus, based on the Elise chassis, was built using the same Lotus software tools used to build the Elise chassis, and is built by the same Norwegian manufacturer. Similarly, the body panels were designed under contract by Barney Hatt of Lotus Design Studios in cooperation with Tesla, but are manufactured from carbon fiber by a third party vendor. Final assembly of the Tesla Roadster is done at Lotus Cars in Hethel, England by both Lotus and Tesla employees. == Lawsuits == In March 2008 it was announced that Tesla had filed a lawsuit against [[Fisker Automotive]], saying that Henrik Fisker "stole design ideas and confidential information related to the design of hybrid and electric cars". Tesla hired Fisker to design the WhiteStar sedan but decided against the design as it was, according to Tesla chairman Elon Musk, "substandard".{{ cite web | url = http://www.autoblog.com/2008/04/15/tesla-files-suit-against-fisker-automotive/ | publisher = Autoblog | title = Tesla files suit against Fisker Automotive }} Also in March 2008, [[Magna International]] filed a lawsuit against Tesla claiming that it was never paid for services rendered. Tesla hired Magna to help design a 2-speed transmission for its Roadster (a transmission they decided not to use).{{ cite web | url = http://www.autoblog.com/2008/04/16/tables-turned-tesla-motors-sued-by-transmission-supplier-magna/ | publisher = Autoblog | title = Tesla Motors Sued By Transmission Supplier Magna }} == Tesla Energy Group == Interest in Tesla Motors' battery technology led to the formation of a division called the '''Tesla Energy Group'''. [[Think Nordic|Think Global]] was to be the first customer, placing an order for development and delivery of battery packs for their electric cars. However, Tesla put their plans on hold due to delays in production and engineering, and Think has accordingly found a new battery supplier, [[EnerDel]].{{cite press release | publisher = [[Tesla Motors]] | date = 2007-05-22 | url = http://www.teslamotors.com/media/press_room.php?id=509 | title = Tesla Energy Group, a New Division of Tesla Motors, Signs Development and Supply Agreement Worth $43 Million with Think of Norway }} == Moniker == Tesla Motors is named after [[Nikola Tesla]], an [[inventor]], [[electrical engineer]] and [[scientist]]. The principal visionary and inventor of the [[alternating current]] [[induction motor]] and [[polyphase system]] of electrical distribution, Tesla's designs and ideas changed the world, providing a stable means of delivering electricity over enormous distances; the ''[[War of Currents]]'' with [[Thomas Edison]] over this issue went on for years with Tesla and [[George Westinghouse]] ultimately triumphing. The Tesla Roadster's power plant is basically a [[three-phase electric power|three-phase]] Tesla [[AC induction motor]]. == Board of Directors == * [[Elon Musk]] - Chairman of the board of directors, former President of [[Paypal]], founder and CEO of [[SpaceX]] * [[Ze'ev Drori]] - CEO of Tesla Motors * [[Ira Ehrenpreis]] - General Partner, [[Technology Partners]] * [[Antonio J. Gracias]] - CEO and Chairman of the Investment Committee at [[Valor Equity Partners]] * [[Jim Marver]] - Co-founder and managing partner, [[VantagePoint Venture Partners]] * [[Kimbal Musk]] - CEO of [[Medium, Inc.]] * [[Larry W. Sonsini]] - Chairman, [[Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati]] * [[Steve Westly]] - Former Controller for California and founder of [[The Westly Group]] == References == {{refs|2}} == External links == * [http://www.teslamotors.com/ Official Tesla Motors website] * [http://edcorner.stanford.edu/authorMaterialInfo.html?mid=1862&author=253 Distribution plan for the new Electric Car], Martin Eberhard speaks at Stanford * [http://www.channel4.com/4car/news/news-story.jsp?news_id=14656 Channel 4 news story] (contains speculative material and rumors) * [http://www.guardian.co.uk/cars/story/0,,1855488,00.html The Guardian newspaper on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_motors_ev.php Tesla Motors: Affordable Electric Cars are Coming] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster.php Tesla Roadster: The Electric Car that Redefines "Power" (Part 1)] * [http://www.treehugger.com/files/2006/08/tesla_roadster2.php Tesla Roadster: New Power to the People (Part 2)] * [http://www.bbc.co.uk/radio4/news/inbusiness/inbusiness_20060921.shtml Plug in Cars] In Business, Radio 4, September 2006 * [http://www.greenvehiclenews.com/content/view/400/28/ Green Vehicle News on the Tesla Roadster] * [http://www.latimes.com/classified/automotive/highway1/la-hy-neil29nov29,0,7930203.story?coll=la-home-headlines Tesla Roadster review] by L.A.
